Title: Junior - Data Scientist
Description:
Stiamo cercando una figura junior da inserire nel team Smart Solutions Lab di Deloitte Business Solution nella sede di Milano, che lavorerà in modalità Hybrid.

Lo Smart Solutions Lab sviluppa piattaforme e soluzioni data-driven altamente personalizzate per i clienti, integrando tecnologie avanzate, automazione e analisi dei dati. La nuova risorsa lavorerà all’interno di un team multidisciplinare e contribuirà allo sviluppo e alla gestione di sistemi basati su dati complessi e processi automatizzati.

Your Role

La risorsa Junior verrà affiancata da profili più senior e, nel tempo, si occuperà di:


Supportare attività di data preparation, trasformazione e integrazione dati.
Sviluppare dashboards per analisi statistiche sui clienti.
Scrivere ed eseguire query SQL per estrarre, unire e analizzare dati da database relazionali.
Documentare procedure, dataset e funzionalità sviluppate.
Lavorare in team con data scientist, data engineer, sviluppatori e consulenti con background diversi.


What we offer

La retribuzione prevista per la posizione di Analyst è compresa tra EUR 25.000 e EUR 28.000 lordi annui.

In caso di stage, è prevista un’indennità di tirocinio.

L'inquadramento verrà definito in fase di selezione sulla base del profilo e in applicazione del CCNL Terziario Distribuzione e Servizi.

La definizione del compenso specifico, inclusi eventuali elementi variabili (come MBO) o benefit accessori, dipenderà dalla valutazione del profilo rispetto ai requisiti della posizione.

Winning Requirements


Laurea triennale o magistrale in discipline STEM, economiche, statistiche o affini.
Esperienza lavorativa complessiva < 1 anno, compresi stage/tirocini.
Esperienza con strumenti di Data Visualization (Power BI, Tableau, Qlik).
Conoscenza delle basi di SQL: join, filtri, aggregazioni, query su database relazionali.
Familiarità con strumenti di analisi e gestione dati.
Capacità di lavorare in team, buona comunicazione e predisposizione al problem solving.
Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese.


Nice to have


Esperienza con servizi cloud (Azure, AWS, Databricks, ecc.)
Conoscenza base di Python: strutture dati, notebook, manipolazione dati (preferibilmente con librerie come pandas).
Conoscenza base di concetti di automazione, AI/ML o di strumenti cloud.
Conoscenza, anche introduttiva, di C# o del framework .NET.
Esperienza con sistemi di versionamento del codice (Git) e ambienti di sviluppo come VS Code.


To Make An Impact That Matters

Il network Deloitte, leader nei servizi professionali alle imprese, opera a livello globale in più di 150 Paesi con oltre 460 mila persone.

In Italia, Deloitte è presente in 24 città con circa 13.400 persone che assistono i clienti offrendo servizi di Audit & Assurance, Technology & Transformantion, Strategy, Risk & Transactions Advisory e Tax & Legal.

In qualità di network globale ci impegniamo per affrontare le principali sfide sociali, economiche e ambientali della società di oggi. WorldImpact è il nostro programma globale che riunisce tutte le iniziative attraverso cui vogliamo avere un impatto positivo sulle persone, i clienti, l’ambiente e le comunità in cui operiamo.

Con Impact for Italy, la declinazione italiana di WorldImpact, Deloitte ha realizzato progetti che - in collaborazione con istituzioni, realtà del terzo settore, università e altri soggetti - hanno lasciato un impatto concreto e una legacy per il futuro su tutto il territorio nazionale.

La ricerca rispetta il d.lgs. 198/2006, il D.lgs. 215/03 e il D.lgs. 216/03 ed è aperta a candidat* di qualsiasi orientamento o espressione di genere, orientamento sessuale, età, etnia e credo religioso. Il presente annuncio è stato ideato nel rispetto della diversity e dell’inclusività.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Are you looking for a new challenge?

Fancy helping us shape the future of motor insurance?

Prima could be the place for you.

Since 2015, we’ve been using our love of data and tech to rethink motor insurance and bring drivers a great experience at a great price. Our story began in Italy, where we’ve quickly become the number one online motor insurance provider. In fact, we’re trusted by over 5 million drivers. And now we’re expanding to help millions more drivers in the UK and Spain.

To help fuel that growth, we need a Data Scientist to join our Italian Pricing & Underwriting team, that is the strategic engine behind Prima's growth.

As part of this team, you’ll play a pivotal role in shaping our strategy and strengthening our competitive edge. Using data, analytical thinking, machine learning and business acumen, you’ll help predict the future and set the right prices, keeping our offering both attractive and sustainable. You’ll work with your data science tools and skills to bring real-world impact, collaborating with cross-functional teams to turn insights into action and fuel innovation across the company.

Excited to make an impact? Here are the details

What You’ll Do


Leverage Machine Learning and advanced data analytics to identify risks and set the right price for each client;
Analyze and interpret complex data to support strategic decision-making;
Contribute directly to the company’s growth and long-term success through actionable insights.


What We’re Looking For


Strong academic background in Mathematics, Physics, Mathematical Engineering, Physical Engineering, Data Science, Informatics, Computer Engineering or Statistics;
All experience levels, including recent graduates with no prior work experience;
Excellent quantitative, logical, and analytical skills;
Curiosity, proactivity, and innovative thinking to drive new ideas and solutions.


Nice-to-have


Possess a solid foundation in code development and machine learning;
Know how to create, manipulate and analyze large datasets.


Curious to know more? Apply or check out this video.

Why you’ll love it here

We want to make Prima a happy and empowering place to work. So if you decide to join us, you can expect plenty of perks.

🤸 Work Your Way: Enjoy full flexibility – work from home, the office or a mix of both. Plus, work from anywhere for up to 30 days a year.

🏁 Grow with us: We may move fast at Prima, but we move together. Get access to learning resources, mentorship and a growth plan tailored to you.

🌈 Thrive and perform: Your best work begins when you feel your best. Enjoy private healthcare, gym discounts, wellbeing programs and mental health support.

Think you’re a match? Apply now.

At Prima, we celebrate uniqueness. If you don’t meet every requirement but are passionate about this role, we still want to hear from you. Innovation thrives on diverse perspectives.

Prima is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Need accommodations during the process? Email us at accessible.recruiting@prima.it. Let’s build the future of insurance, together.

Title: Data Scientist
HTML:
📍 Sede: Milano (ibrido) | 🕐 Contratto a tempo indeterminato | 🍽 Ticket Restaurant + Welfare


MCA Groupe, multinazionale francese di consulenza ingegneristica ed informatica, è nata a Parigi nel 1991. Oggi è presente in 7 Paesi in Europa: Italia, Francia, Belgio, Germania, Olanda, Svezia e Spagna.

Con oltre 1500 talenti nel mondo e una cultura orientata alla crescita, ci posizioniamo come partner strategico per i principali player in settori ad alto contenuto tecnologico.

MCA Italia, con sedi a Torino, Milano e Roma, è attiva nei settori Mobility & Digital e conta oltre 190 professionisti.


🎯 Siamo alla ricerca di un Data Scientist & Machine Learning Engineer


La figura sarà inserita nell’area Data di un cliente, contribuendo allo sviluppo di modelli predittivi, sistemi di raccomandazione e soluzioni di ottimizzazione per supportare decisioni e strategie data-driven. Lavorerà su progetti innovativi ad alto impatto, collaborando con team multidisciplinari per trasformare dati complessi in soluzioni concrete a supporto del business.


💻 L’attività prevede:


• Analisi ed elaborazione di grandi volumi di dati provenienti da diverse fonti;

• Sviluppo, validazione e monitoraggio di modelli di forecasting e predictive analytics;

• Progettazione e implementazione di recommendation systems per casi d’uso business-oriented;

• Sviluppo di algoritmi di Machine Learning supervisionato e non supervisionato;

• Realizzazione di modelli di ottimizzazione matematica e programmazione lineare per la massimizzazione di KPI soggetti a vincoli operativi e di business;

• Gestione dell’intero ciclo di vita dei modelli, dall’analisi esplorativa dei dati al deployment e monitoraggio delle performance;

• Collaborazione con stakeholder business e team tecnici per l'identificazione e la realizzazione di soluzioni data-driven.


⚡ Competenze tecniche:


• Esperienza nello sviluppo di modelli di Machine Learning applicati a casi d’uso reali;

• Ottima conoscenza di Python e delle principali librerie di Data Science e Machine Learning;

• Esperienza nella realizzazione di modelli di forecasting e predictive analytics;

• Conoscenza ed esperienza nello sviluppo di recommendation systems;

• Competenze in statistica, modellazione matematica e analisi quantitativa;

• Esperienza con algoritmi di ottimizzazione matematica, programmazione lineare e ricerca operativa;


🎯 Il profilo che cerchiamo:


• Esperienza di almeno 2-3 anni in ambito Data Science o Machine Learning;

• Laurea in Informatica, Ingegneria, Matematica, Statistica, Fisica o discipline affini;

• Approccio analitico e data-driven;

• Capacità di lavorare in team e di interfacciarsi con stakeholder tecnici e business.


🤝 Quello che ti offriamo:


• Range retributivo per profili da 3 a 5 anni di esperienza, definito in base alle competenze tecniche e all’esperienza maturata: €31.000-€37.000

• Ticket buoni pasto da 10€ e Welfare basato sulla seniority aziendale

• Un Career Path definito attraverso criteri oggettivi e verificabili, che ti accompagnerà nella crescita professionale in MCA Italia

• Ambiente internazionale e cultura aziendale orientata all'innovazione e alla collaborazione


💡 Non ti riconosci in tutti i requisiti?


In MCA Italia crediamo nel potenziale delle persone e nella crescita continua. Se il ruolo ti interessa e pensi di poter portare valore con le tue competenze e la tua motivazione, ti invitiamo comunque a candidarti, anche se non rispecchi al 100% i requisiti indicati.


⚖️ Pari Opportunità, Privacy e Trasparenza


Promuoviamo una cultura aziendale fondata sul rispetto, sull’inclusione e sulla valorizzazione delle persone. Nel processo di selezione le candidature sono valutate esclusivamente sulla base di competenze, esperienze e attitudini professionali, senza alcuna discriminazione, diretta o indiretta, legata a genere (L. 903/77 e L. 125/91), età, origine etnica, orientamento sessuale o altre caratteristiche non rilevanti ai fini della valutazione.


I dati personali raccolti sono trattati con riservatezza e nel rispetto della normativa vigente in materia di protezione dei dati personali, del Regolamento (UE) 2016/679, delle procedure interne e del Codice Etico aziendale.


Le persone candidate sono invitate a consultare l’Informativa Privacy, ai sensi degli artt. 13 e 14 del GDPR, disponibile al seguente link:


https://mca-website.cdn.prismic.io/mca-website/aCG1jSdWJ-7kR6kA_MGR-INF-RM002-ENGDataProtectionPolicy.pdf


L’annuncio è rivolto anche a candidat* appartenenti alle categorie protette ai sensi degli artt. 1 e 18 della L. 68/99.

Title: Junior Data Scientist - Milano
HTML:
Dale Consulting ricerca Junior Data Scientist per un importante cliente internazionale, da inserire in un team strutturato e tecnologicamente avanzato.


| CHI SIAMO

Dale Consulting, società di Consulenza Informatica, offre servizi ai principali player internazionali in ambito tecnologico, supportando le aziende nella realizzazione e gestione di soluzioni innovative e ad alto valore strategico.

Società dedita all’eccellenza e all'impegno costante per supportare le sfide dei propri clienti, fornendo loro soluzioni customizzate e contribuendo alla realizzazione e gestione di prodotti, servizi e strumenti di alto valore strategico.


| DI COSA TI OCCUPERAI

Entrerai in un contesto enterprise ad alta complessità, dove avrai l’opportunità di crescere e sviluppare competenze in ambito Data Science e AI, lavorando a stretto contatto con professionisti senior.


Sarai coinvolto in attività di analisi dati, sviluppo di modelli e supporto alla realizzazione di soluzioni data-driven.


In particolare ti occuperai di:


  • Supportare lo sviluppo di modelli di machine learning;
  • Analizzare ed elaborare dataset;
  • Scrivere codice in Python per attività di analisi e modellazione;
  • Collaborare con il team nello sviluppo e nell’integrazione delle soluzioni;
  • Partecipare alle attività di validazione e testing dei modelli;
  • Contribuire alla documentazione tecnica.


| COSA RICHIEDIAMO

  • Laurea in discipline STEM (Informatica, Ingegneria, Matematica, Fisica o affini);
  • Conoscenza base di Python;
  • Familiarità con concetti di analisi dati e machine learning;
  • Conoscenza base di SQL;
  • Interesse per il mondo dei dati e dell’intelligenza artificiale.


| NICE TO HAVE

  • Progetti accademici o personali in ambito Data Science;
  • Conoscenza di librerie Python (Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn);
  • Familiarità con strumenti di data visualization;
  • Esperienze di stage o tirocinio in ambito dati.


| COSA OFFRIAMO?

  • Percorso di crescita strutturato con affiancamento a figure senior;
  • Coinvolgimento in progetti complessi e innovativi;
  • Ambiente dinamico e orientato all’apprendimento.


Modalità di lavoro: ibrida (3+2) – Milano

Contratto a tempo indeterminato e RAL commisurata alla reale esperienza


L’offerta si rivolge a candidati ambosessi, nel rispetto del Codice delle pari opportunità D.Lgs. 11 aprile 2006, N. 198 e ss.mm.ii.

Title: Clinical Data Scientist
Description:
Fondazione Don Carlo Gnocchi ETS (www.dongnocchi.it), realtà sanitaria e socio sanitaria con 3.700 posti letto, svolge attività di cura, riabilitazione, assistenza e integrazione sociale, in regime di accreditamento con il Servizio Sanitario Nazionale in 25 Strutture distribuite in 9 regioni, rispondendo anche con soluzioni innovative e sperimentali ai bisogni emergenti delle persone più fragili. Riconosciuta Istituto di Ricovero e Cura a Carattere Scientifico (IRCCS), conta circa 6.000 operatori tra personale dipendente e collaboratori professionali.




Fondazione Don Gnocchi ricerca una/un Clinical Data Scientist, da inserire presso l’IRCCS – S. Maria Nascente di Milano. Ricerchiamo una persona interessata alle applicazioni dell'intelligenza artificiale e al machine learning nel settore sanitario, con esperienza nell'utilizzo di tecniche di analisi dei dati strutturati e testuali e nella realizzazione di report e dashboard informative.




Responsabilità

partecipare a progetti di ricerca clinica e scientifica, supportando la progettazione e la gestione di studi osservazionali e sperimentali;
collaborare nella raccolta, gestione, analisi e interpretazione dei dati di ricerca, contribuendo alla produzione di evidenze a supporto delle attività scientifiche e organizzative;
supportare lo sviluppo di soluzioni data-driven attraverso l'utilizzo di strumenti di analisi statistica e metodologie quantitative;
contribuire alla realizzazione di report, dashboard informative e documentazione scientifica destinata a stakeholder interni ed esterni;
collaborare con professionisti sanitari, ricercatori e figure tecniche nell'ambito di progetti di innovazione, digitalizzazione e miglioramento dei processi;
supportare le attività di analisi dei dati strutturati e testuali, anche mediante strumenti di intelligenza artificiale e machine learning applicati al contesto sanitario;
garantire la qualità, la correttezza e la conformità del trattamento dei dati nel rispetto delle normative vigenti in materia di privacy e sicurezza delle informazioni.




Requisiti




Tirolo di studio

Laurea magistrale in Ingegneria Biomedica, Ingegneria Informatica, Data Science, Informatica, Statistica o discipline affini.

Ricerca Clinica e Scientifica

Conoscenza delle metodologie di ricerca clinica e scientifica.
Conoscenza delle principali tecniche di raccolta, gestione e analisi dei dati di ricerca.
Capacità di analisi e interpretazione dei dati.
Conoscenza dei principi di analisi statistica.
Capacità di consultazione e revisione della letteratura scientifica.

Data Analysis & Digital Health

Conoscenza di strumenti e metodologie per la gestione e l’organizzazione dei dati.
Conoscenza di linguaggi di programmazione quali Python, SQL e SPSS.
Interesse per l’applicazione dell’intelligenza artificiale e del machine learning in ambito sanitario.
Interesse per i processi di innovazione, interoperabilità e digitalizzazione in sanità;
Conoscenza della normativa GDPR e dell'AI Act.

Soft Skills

Capacità analitica e orientamento al problem solving.
Predisposizione al lavoro in team multidisciplinari.
Capacità organizzative e di gestione delle priorità.
Capacità comunicative e di divulgazione scientifica.
Attitudine all’apprendimento.




Previsto inserimento con contratto co.co.co. di un anno.




Range retributivo (RAL): €27.000 - €30.000




Il presente annuncio è rivolto ad entrambi i sessi (L.903/77) e a soggetti di tutte le età e nazionalità purché abbiano una buona conoscenza della lingua italiana.




Le risorse interessate ad approfondire le opportunità di impiego possono indirizzare il proprio CV esclusivamente in via telematica autorizzando esplicitamente la Fondazione al trattamento dei dati personali (D. Lgs. 196/2003 e GDPR 679/16).

  • Predisposizione al lavoro in team multidisciplinari.
  • Capacità organizzative e di gestione delle priorità.
  • Capacità comunicative e di divulgazione scientifica.
  • Attitudine all’apprendimento.


Previsto inserimento con contratto co.co.co. di un anno.


Range retributivo (RAL): €27.000 - €30.000


Il presente annuncio è rivolto ad entrambi i sessi (L.903/77) e a soggetti di tutte le età e nazionalità purché abbiano una buona conoscenza della lingua italiana.


Le risorse interessate ad approfondire le opportunità di impiego possono indirizzare il proprio CV esclusivamente in via telematica autorizzando esplicitamente la Fondazione al trattamento dei dati personali (D. Lgs. 196/2003 e GDPR 679/16).

Title: Data Science Specialist
Description:
Founded in 1913 in Italy, the Prada Group was built on a tradition of excellence and with a vision of innovation. The Group, a world leader in the luxury sector, operates in more than 45 countries with the PRADA, Miu Miu, Versace, Church’s, Car Shoe and Luna Rossa brands, and has employees of over 100 nationalities.

Job Purpose

We are seeking a Data Science Specialist to join the Data & Analytics Team within IT. Based in our Milan HQ, the role focuses on designing and industrializing AI solutions for customer personalization and recommendation systems. The ideal candidate combines strong technical skills with the ability to coordinate small external development teams and work across multiple IT and business functions on complex enterprise programs.

Responsibilities


Design and deploy personalization models and recommendation engines, translating business requirements into scalable, data-driven solutions.
Lead the full lifecycle of data science projects — from prototyping to production — ensuring alignment with enterprise platform standards.
Coordinate small teams of external developers, overseeing code quality, timelines, and deliverables.
Collaborate on cross-IT programs, managing dependencies with other teams (Data Platform, Integration, Digital, CRM) and business stakeholders.
Build and maintain scalable data pipelines on Databricks and Azure to support model training, serving, and continuous improvement.
Monitor model performance and proactively identify opportunities to enhance accuracy and business impact.
Contribute to ML Ops best practices and reusable frameworks within the Data Science area.


Knowledge And Skills


Master’s degree in a quantitative field (Statistics, Computer Science, Machine Learning, Data Science, Mathematics, Engineering) or equivalent experience.
4+ years of industry experience in data science, with a strong focus on personalization and recommendation systems.
Advanced proficiency in Python and PySpark; solid knowledge of SQL.
Hands-on experience with recommendation models (collaborative filtering, content-based, hybrid) and personalization algorithms at scale.
Strong experience with Databricks (MLflow, Feature Store, Model Serving) and Azure.
Familiarity with ML Ops practices (CI/CD for ML, model monitoring, automated retraining).
Experience managing external development teams and ensuring delivery quality.
Proven ability to work on complex, cross-functional programs in international environments.
Experience with Agile methodologies (Scrum, Kanban) is preferred.
Previous experience in Fashion & Luxury or management consulting is a plus.
Excellent communication skills; ability to bridge technical and business language.
Fluency in Italian and English, written and spoken.


Joining our Company means working in a creative and international environment, with teams of people motivated by curiosity and the quest for excellence. The engine of our success is the importance and value that we place on the talent and passion of our people leading to their own professional growth.

Prada Group promotes an inclusive work environment, ensuring equal opportunities for all candidates, regardless of gender, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability, or other personal characteristics. We believe that diversity is a value and we are committed to build a fair and respectful workplace for all.

Title: Data Scientist - Fraud Detection
Description:
Are you looking for a new challenge?

Fancy helping us shape the future of motor insurance?

Prima could be the place for you.

Since 2015, we’ve been using our love of data and tech to rethink motor insurance and bring drivers a great experience at a great price. Our story began in Italy, where we’ve quickly become the number one online motor insurance provider. In fact, we’re trusted by over 5 million drivers. And now we’re expanding to help millions more drivers in the UK and Spain.

To help fuel that growth, we need a Data Scientist to join our Anti Fraud Team.

In this role, you’ll have the opportunity to tackle fraud, one of the most impactful and fast-evolving challenges in insurance. You will do this from a global perspective. In fact, the team is responsible for detecting fraudulent claims across all Prima markets: the UK, Spain and Italy.

You’ll collaborate with engineers, claims handlers and business leaders to improve the detection of fraud, directly influencing real-world fraud decisions with impact on the company’s bottom line measured in millions. This is a highly practical area of work where strong business judgment is just as important as technical expertise.

Excited to make an impact? Here are the details

What You’ll Do


Design and improve fraud detection systems that prioritize high-risk claims, optimizing the trade-off between detection accuracy and operational efficiency
Collaborate across teams and functions, working closely with fraud analysts, engineers, and product managers
Develop deep expertise in fraud dynamics, identifying emerging fraud patterns, understanding how they evolve across the insurance lifecycle, and translating these insights into effective detection and prevention strategies, for example identifying suspicious networks of related claims, synthetic entities, or anomalous behavioral patterns


What We’re Looking For


Extensive experience (3+ years) in data science or applied machine learning, leading end-to-end initiatives in complex technical domains and delivering measurable business impact
Strong analytical and quantitative mindset able to translate data into insights, assess model performance, and guide decision-making with evidence and clarity
Structured problem solver skilled at framing ambiguous challenges, defining success metrics, and communicating results to both technical and non-technical audiences
Strong Python programming skills, especially in the data domain, and fluent use of Git
Knowledge of Spark, SQL or any other data-oriented programming language and proficiency with standard data wrangling and modeling libraries (Pandas, Sklearn, Keras, XGBoost, LightGBM, etc.)


Nice-to-Have


Experience in analytical fraud detection in the Insurance, Finance or Law Enforcement domain
In-depth knowledge of graph databases (Neo4j, AWS Neptune) and analytics (NetworkX, GNN) or explainability applied to anomaly detection
Knowledge of BI tools (Tableau or similar)


Why you’ll love it here

We want to make Prima a happy and empowering place to work. So if you decide to join us, you can expect plenty of perks.

🤸 Work Your Way: Enjoy hybrid working, with a mix of home and office days. Plus, for up to 30 days per year, work from anywhere.

🏁 Grow with us: We may move fast at Prima, but we move together. Get access to learning resources, mentorship and a growth plan tailored to you.

🌈 Thrive and perform: Your best work begins when you feel your best. Enjoy private healthcare, gym discounts, wellbeing programs and mental health support.

Think you’re a match? Apply now.

At Prima, we celebrate uniqueness. If you don’t meet every requirement but are passionate about this role, we still want to hear from you. Innovation thrives on diverse perspectives.

Prima is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Need accommodations during the process? Email us at accessible.recruiting@prima.it. Let’s build the future of insurance, together.

Title: Data Scientist
HTML:
PTE consulting è una società di consulenza aziendale che accompagna i propri clienti nel loro percorso di Digital Transformation, sviluppando soluzioni di business e sistemi IT per migliorare i processi aziendali nelle aree AFC, Treasury, Supply Chain e Data Intelligence.

Lavoriamo con importanti clienti internazionali guidandoli nell'innovazione dei processi aziendali, progettando nuove soluzioni che consentano di gestire le prossime sfide tecnologiche e raggiungere il successo.

Per il potenziamento della nostra area di competenza Data & Analytics siamo alla ricerca di Data Scientist con almeno 3 anni di esperienza nell'analisi avanzata dei dati e nello sviluppo di modelli predittivi e machine learning in ambiente Databricks.

La figura inserita sarà coinvolta in progetti per clienti internazionali - operanti principalmente nel settore industriale - e lavorerà all'interno di team strutturati, occupandosi di attività progettuali legate alla gestione, trasformazione e valorizzazione dei dati.

In particolare, si occuperà di:

  • analisi di dati complessi provenienti da diverse sorgenti;
  • sviluppo di modelli statistici, predittivi e di machine learning;
  • identificazione di insight a supporto delle decisioni di business;
  • attività di data preparation, data cleaning e feature engineering;
  • validazione, monitoraggio e ottimizzazione dei modelli sviluppati;
  • utilizzo di linguaggi e strumenti per l'analisi dei dati, come Python, SQL e principali librerie di data science;
  • collaborazione con team funzionali e tecnici per tradurre le esigenze di business in soluzioni analitiche;
  • redazione di report, dashboard e documentazione tecnica a supporto delle attività progettuali.

Requirements

La ricerca è rivolta a profili professionali con esperienza in ambito Data Science, maturata su progetti di analisi avanzata, modellazione statistica, machine learning o sviluppo di soluzioni data-driven.

La candidatura ideale ha sviluppato una buona conoscenza delle principali tecniche di analisi dati, modellazione predittiva e machine learning, nonché competenze nell'utilizzo di Python e SQL per l'analisi, la trasformazione e la valorizzazione dei dati. Costituisce elemento di interesse la familiarità con ambienti cloud/data platform, in particolare Databricks, PySpark e strumenti a supporto del ciclo di vita dei modelli, come MLflow. È inoltre apprezzata la capacità di sviluppare dashboard, strumenti di reporting e analisi a supporto dei processi decisionali.

È richiesto il possesso di una laurea in discipline STEM, una buona padronanza della lingua inglese e la capacità di relazionarsi con interlocutori tecnici e funzionali.

Oltre alle competenze tecniche, cerchiamo colleghi che condividano il nostro approccio e modo di lavorare: persone motivate e orientate al lavoro in team, con capacità di iniziativa e voglia di contribuire all'identificazione di soluzioni innovative, che desiderano partecipare al successo di una società in espansione che offre ampi spazi di crescita professionale.

Benefits

In aggiunta alla retribuzione fissa sono previsti una componente bonus (variabile in base a risultati aziendali e performance individuali), buoni pasto, assicurazione sanitaria aziendale, percorsi di formazione e certificazioni finanziati dall'azienda.

Lavoriamo prevalentemente in smart working/da remoto, con sedi di riferimento a Milano, Roma o Pescara.

Ti troverai in un ambiente giovane e collaborativo, che promuove una cultura che valorizza diversità, equità e inclusione.

Title: Data Scientist Intern
Description:
Chi Siamo

The Newco è una data & technology company nata per abilitare la crescita di business digitali attraverso l’uso strategico dei dati, delle tecnologie cloud e dell’intelligenza artificiale. Collaboriamo con aziende leader nei settori media, retail e finance, supportandole nello sviluppo di soluzioni data-driven per decisioni più efficaci e strategie di marketing più performanti.




Il Ruolo

Per ampliare il nostro team Data & Analytics, stiamo cercando un* Data Scientist Intern appassionato di analisi dati e delle nuove frontiere dell'Intelligenza Artificiale.

Affiancherai i nostri esperti in un percorso focalizzato sull'estrazione di valore dai dati, esplorando non solo l'analisi descrittiva e statistica, ma anche l'applicazione di tecnologie innovative basate su AI Generativa e ricerca semantica. È un'opportunità pensata per chi vuole sporcarsi le mani su dataset reali e scoprire come le moderne architetture di dati stiano trasformando il business.

📍 Location: La sede principale è a Milano (5 min a piedi dalla fermata Sondrio), ma operiamo in modalità ibrida, offrendo la flessibilità necessaria per bilanciare ufficio e smart working.




Cosa farai nel dettaglio

In affiancamento al tuo tutor e al team Data & Analytics, ti occuperai di:

Supportare l'esplorazione e l'analisi dei dati di performance digitali per individuare trend e insight rilevanti.
Apprendere la gestione di grandi volumi di dati su BigQuery, ottimizzando query per l'estrazione di informazioni complesse.
Approfondire le tecniche di Data Science moderna, collaborando alla sperimentazione su embedding e ricerca vettoriale per il recupero intelligente delle informazioni.
Contribuire alla creazione di dashboard interattive e strumenti di monitoraggio per rendere i dati accessibili e azionabili.
Supportare il team nella prototipazione di soluzioni che sfruttano la Google Cloud Platform per l'elaborazione dei dati.




Cosa Cerchiamo

Cerchiamo una persona con una forte curiosità tecnologica e solide basi analitiche:

Percorso accademico: Laurea (conseguita o in corso) in Discipline STEM (Statistica, Matematica, Informatica, Ingegneria, Fisica) o Data Science.
Competenze tecniche di base: Buona conoscenza di Python e delle librerie standard per la manipolazione dei dati (Pandas, Numpy).
Fondamentali SQL: Capacità di scrivere query per interrogare database (la conoscenza di contesti cloud come BigQuery è un plus).
Interesse per l'AI moderna: Curiosità verso concetti come Vector Search, Embedding e LLM, e voglia di capire come applicarli a casi d'uso reali.
Soft Skills: Approccio analitico, attenzione ai dettagli, spirito collaborativo e attitudine al problem solving.
Lingue: Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese.




Cosa offriamo

Stage formativo con rimborso spese competitivo.
Mentorship dedicata: imparerai i segreti del mestiere da professionisti esperti.
Tecnologie all'avanguardia: lavorerai su progetti che integrano le ultime novità di Google Cloud (Vertex AI, BigQuery, Vector Search).
Ambiente stimolante: un contesto giovane dove la curiosità intellettuale viene sempre premiata.
Flessibilità: Approccio orientato ai risultati con possibilità di smart working.




Retribuzione: a partire da 800€ / mese

Non vediamo l’ora di conoscerti. Candidati ora o scrivici per saperne di più!

Title: GROWTH DATA SCIENTIST
Description:
WHAT IS EDENRED?




Edenred is a global leader in the employee benefits sector, offering companies digital solutions dedicated to improve the well-being of people at work.




A digital platform that connects 60 million users and over 2 million affiliated businesses in 44 countries through about 1 million corporate clients. In Italy, the ecosystem involves 3 million users, over 180.000 corporate clients and more than 300.000 affiliated establishments, making its network the most widespread, present in every Italian country.




Edenred’s solutions improve quality of life and strengthen employee engagement—from lunch breaks to leisure time, personal care, and sustainable mobility. Meal vouchers, shopping vouchers and corporate welfare tools are tailored to all types of organizations and employees. They support HR departments, make companies more attractive and efficient, and stimulate both the labor market and the local economy.




True to the Group’s purpose “Enrich connections. For good.”, these solutions develop and enrich connections that empower businesses and promote inclusive solutions, placing people, the environment, and local communities at the center through a concrete ESG commitment.




Edenred’s 12,000 employees are dedicated every day to making the world of work a more connected, safer, more efficient, and more responsible ecosystem.




In 2024, thanks to its global technology assets, the Group managed close to €45 billion in business volume, primarily carried out via mobile applications, online platforms and cards.




Edenred is listed on the Euronext Paris stock exchange and included in the following indices: CAC Large 60, Euronext 100, Euronext Tech Leaders, FTSE4Good, DJSI Europe Index, DJSI World Index, and MSCI Europe.




YOU WILL VIBE WITH US




We are looking for a Growth Data Scientist with proven experience in developing Advanced Analytics and Machine Learning solutions to support business processes, particularly in the areas of Sales and Customer Management.

The successful candidate will work closely with business stakeholders, transforming data and insights into concrete actions while contributing to the adoption of models within operational processes.

This role requires a strong combination of technical expertise and business acumen, operating in a hybrid environment that combines model development with their practical application across business functions.




Key Responsibilities:




Design, develop, and deploy Machine Learning and Advanced Analytics models
Build predictive models (e.g., churn, cross-sell, up-sell, propensity models, etc.) and develop the related features and datasets
Translate models into operational outputs (customer lists, scores, targeting logics) to support commercial processes
Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into data-driven solutions
Monitor model performance and business impact, contributing to a test-and-learn approach
Present insights, results, and strategic recommendations to management
Work closely with Data Engineering and IT teams to ensure data quality, governance, and availability
Contribute to the evolution of the company’s data-driven strategy




WE WILL VIBE WITH YOU




Passion for customer, respect, imagination, simplicity and entrepreneurial spirit are the values we look for in our people. If you recognize yourself and want to get involved in a stimulating and multinational environment, send us your application and become an Edenreder too!




Minimum 3–5 years of experience in Data Scientist roles, preferably within Sales or Customer Analytics
Strong knowledge of Python, PySpark, SQL, and major Machine Learning libraries (e.g., scikit-learn, XGBoost, TensorFlow, or similar)
Hands-on experience with Azure Databricks (including Workflows and Job Orchestration) and familiarity with the Azure ecosystem: Azure Machine Learning, Azure DevOps, Azure Data Factory
Practical experience in developing predictive models with end-to-end project ownership (e.g., propensity, churn, segmentation)
Ability to work with complex and/or incomplete datasets
Good understanding of data architectures and ETL/ELT pipelines for data preparation and transformation
Degree in a scientific or quantitative discipline
Fluent English




Soft Skills & Qualifications




Strong interest in applying analytical models to real business contexts (particularly Sales and Marketing, though not exclusively)
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, including interaction with senior management
Strong storytelling and presentation skills for both technical and non-technical audiences
Ability to interpret data strategically and with a results-oriented mindset
Ability to translate models and analyses into actionable business outputs




Nice to Have




Experience with Data Visualization tools (Power BI or similar)
Experience in CRM and campaign management environments
Familiarity with dbt, Airflow, and development environments such us VS Code




OTHER DETAILS




Permanent Contract
CCNL Commercio

HTML:
WHAT IS EDENRED?


Edenred is a global leader in the employee benefits sector, offering companies digital solutions dedicated to improve the well-being of people at work.


A digital platform that connects 60 million users and over 2 million affiliated businesses in 44 countries through about 1 million corporate clients. In Italy, the ecosystem involves 3 million users, over 180.000 corporate clients and more than 300.000 affiliated establishments, making its network the most widespread, present in every Italian country.


Edenred’s solutions improve quality of life and strengthen employee engagement—from lunch breaks to leisure time, personal care, and sustainable mobility. Meal vouchers, shopping vouchers and corporate welfare tools are tailored to all types of organizations and employees. They support HR departments, make companies more attractive and efficient, and stimulate both the labor market and the local economy.


True to the Group’s purpose “Enrich connections. For good.”, these solutions develop and enrich connections that empower businesses and promote inclusive solutions, placing people, the environment, and local communities at the center through a concrete ESG commitment.


Edenred’s 12,000 employees are dedicated every day to making the world of work a more connected, safer, more efficient, and more responsible ecosystem.


In 2024, thanks to its global technology assets, the Group managed close to €45 billion in business volume, primarily carried out via mobile applications, online platforms and cards.


Edenred is listed on the Euronext Paris stock exchange and included in the following indices: CAC Large 60, Euronext 100, Euronext Tech Leaders, FTSE4Good, DJSI Europe Index, DJSI World Index, and MSCI Europe.


YOU WILL VIBE WITH US


We are looking for a Growth Data Scientist with proven experience in developing Advanced Analytics and Machine Learning solutions to support business processes, particularly in the areas of Sales and Customer Management.

The successful candidate will work closely with business stakeholders, transforming data and insights into concrete actions while contributing to the adoption of models within operational processes.

This role requires a strong combination of technical expertise and business acumen, operating in a hybrid environment that combines model development with their practical application across business functions.


Key Responsibilities:


  • Design, develop, and deploy Machine Learning and Advanced Analytics models
  • Build predictive models (e.g., churn, cross-sell, up-sell, propensity models, etc.) and develop the related features and datasets
  • Translate models into operational outputs (customer lists, scores, targeting logics) to support commercial processes
  • Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into data-driven solutions
  • Monitor model performance and business impact, contributing to a test-and-learn approach
  • Present insights, results, and strategic recommendations to management
  • Work closely with Data Engineering and IT teams to ensure data quality, governance, and availability
  • Contribute to the evolution of the company’s data-driven strategy


WE WILL VIBE WITH YOU


Passion for customer, respect, imagination, simplicity and entrepreneurial spirit are the values we look for in our people. If you recognize yourself and want to get involved in a stimulating and multinational environment, send us your application and become an Edenreder too!


  • Minimum 3–5 years of experience in Data Scientist roles, preferably within Sales or Customer Analytics
  • Strong knowledge of Python, PySpark, SQL, and major Machine Learning libraries (e.g., scikit-learn, XGBoost, TensorFlow, or similar)
  • Hands-on experience with Azure Databricks (including Workflows and Job Orchestration) and familiarity with the Azure ecosystem: Azure Machine Learning, Azure DevOps, Azure Data Factory
  • Practical experience in developing predictive models with end-to-end project ownership (e.g., propensity, churn, segmentation)
  • Ability to work with complex and/or incomplete datasets
  • Good understanding of data architectures and ETL/ELT pipelines for data preparation and transformation
  • Degree in a scientific or quantitative discipline
  • Fluent English


Soft Skills & Qualifications


  • Strong interest in applying analytical models to real business contexts (particularly Sales and Marketing, though not exclusively)
  • Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, including interaction with senior management
  • Strong storytelling and presentation skills for both technical and non-technical audiences
  • Ability to interpret data strategically and with a results-oriented mindset
  • Ability to translate models and analyses into actionable business outputs


Nice to Have


  • Experience with Data Visualization tools (Power BI or similar)
  • Experience in CRM and campaign management environments
  • Familiarity with dbt, Airflow, and development environments such us VS Code


OTHER DETAILS


  • Permanent Contract
  • CCNL Commercio
  • Gross Annual Salary (RAL) starting from € 38.000, determined on the basis of objective and transparent criteria, including professional experience gained, specialized technical skills, the level of autonomy and responsibility required, and alignment with the company's compensation and job classification framework
  • Performance bonus in line with the company's incentive plan

Title: Graduate data scientist
Description:
At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards.

In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other.

For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate.

A few examples of your responsibilities
Design and improve data pipelines. Contribute to the design and evolution of data systems—ensuring data is accurate, reliable, and efficiently processed across diverse tech stacks.
Ensure data correctness and scalability. Work closely with software engineers to validate data, define metrics, and improve the robustness of reporting and analytics systems.
Develop internal data products and tooling. Help build scalable solutions (e.g., reporting engines, forecasting tools) used by product teams across the company.
Support platform-wide decision-making. Partner with multiple teams to provide the analytical and modeling backbone for business planning and performance tracking.
Leverage AI to accelerate work and uncover insights. Use AI tools to move faster, surface ideas and opportunities, and study competing products and trends.


What we look for
Reasoning ability. Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter.
Drive. You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar.
Team spirit. You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent.
Proficiency in English. You read, write, and speak proficiently in English.


What we offer
Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams. You’ll work in small, result-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field.
An exceptional opportunity for growth. We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too.
Competitive pay and access to equity in the company. Typically, we offer individuals with limited experience an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut.
All. These. Benefits. Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top-of-the-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work.


Commitment & contract
Permanent or fixed-term. Full-time or part-time.

Location
Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland), or fully remote from eligible countries.

The selection process
In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. There are no preferential paths beyond what your application demonstrates, and we evaluate every candidate through the same process.

Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self-reject.

All applications go through our careers page, which is the only way to be considered. If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours. To learn more about what to expect throughout the selection process, you can find additional information here.

We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times.

We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form.

Before you apply
If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again.

Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment.

To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we expect you to spend most days in our Milan office during your first few months with us, regardless of your long-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from one of our offices, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage.

If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

Title: Customer Data Scientist & AI Consultant
HTML:
Trasforma le tue aspirazioni professionali in una storia di successo, entra in PwC come:

Customer Data Scientist & AI Consultant

Your Perspectives

Ti unirai al gruppo di Customer di PwC su sfidanti progetti di Customer Advance Analytics, Data Science e Generative AI in ambito Consumer Markets, Financial Services, Energy&Utilities e Telco&Media, per supportare le organizzazioni nel valorizzare il patrimonio informativo sui propri Clienti e Consumatori finali, utilizzando i dati a disposizione per sviluppare soluzioni innovative basate su AI.

Grazie all’accesso alle vaste competenze del nostro network internazionale, potrai dare spazio alle tue idee e applicare su progettualità concrete le tue conoscenze analitiche fornendo il tuo contributo innovativo.

Sarai coinvolto con crescenti responsabilità nelle seguenti attività:

  • Disegno, sviluppo, test e misurazione delle performance di modelli di Machine Learning e di modelli statistici in ambito Marketing: segmentazioni, Customer scoring, modelli di classificazione, modelli predittivi, modelli di propensione/raccomandazione;

  • Sviluppo e implementazione di tecniche di Generative AI per la creazione di soluzioni conversazionali innovative, utilizzando tecnologie basate su Large Language Model (LLM) e framework avanzati come il Retrieval Augmented Generator (RAG).

  • Analisi dati avanzate sul comportamento dei Clienti sia su canali fisici che digitali attraverso esplorazione di dati strutturati e non strutturati, al fine di ottenere insight di business;

  • Attività di targeting evoluto e valutazione quantitativo-statistica delle campagne di Marketing;

  • Raccolta dei requisiti progettuali a diretto contatto con il cliente per il disegno e l’elaborazione di soluzioni data-driven;

Your unique strengths

  • Stai concludendo il percorso di studi o possiedi una laurea magistrale preferibilmente ad indirizzo scientifico (es. Data Science, Informatica, Statistica, Matematica, Fisica);

  • Hai un’ottima conoscenza del linguaggio Python;

  • Possiedi una buona conoscenza dei database relazionali e del linguaggio SQL;

  • Hai forti competenze analitiche e ti interessa usare i dati per trovare soluzioni funzionali, creative ed innovative a problemi complessi;

  • Parli e scrivi in inglese fluentemente;

  • Ti interessa lavorare in team e crescere in un ambiente di lavoro stimolante e positivo;

  • Sei flessibile e hai una forte motivazione ad intraprendere una carriera professionale in consulenza;

Saranno considerate un plus, le conoscenze, anche a livello base di:

  • Framework Spark e piattaforme Cloud come Amazon Web Services, Azure e Google Cloud Platform;

  • Conoscenza di tecnologie basate su Large Language Model (LLM) e tecniche di Generative AI (RAG o PEFT) per lo sviluppo di applicazioni; nonché sperimentazione di framework agentici più avanzati (LangChain, AutoGen).

  • Tool di versioning come Git; web framework in Python per lo sviluppo di applicazioni web e RESTful APIs (Flask, FastAPI); tecnologie di containerizzazione (Docker) per il rilascio agile di applicazioni web su ambienti Cloud.

  • Artificial Intelligence in ambito Natural Language Processing, Speech Analytics o Computer Vision;

  • Conoscenza ed esperienza di utilizzo di sistemi di business intelligence e dashboarding sia low-code (PowerBI, Tableau) sia code-based per dashboard interattive (Streamlit, Gradio)

Set your expectation higher:  

Il tuo talento e la tua curiosità avranno tutto lo spazio che meritano e, attraverso l’identificazione delle migliori risorse, contribuirai a garantire la qualità dei servizi che offriamo ai nostri clienti. In questo percorso di crescita sarai accompagnato da colleghi esperti e da una formazione continua e su misura grazie alla quale potrai mettere le tue competenze a disposizione dei clienti interni.  
  

 Feel at home:  

 Ci prendiamo cura delle nostre persone mettendole nelle condizioni di crescere ed esprimere al meglio il proprio potenziale. Ci impegniamo a creare valore per i clienti, le persone e la comunità, nel rispetto dell'ambiente.  

 

GIVE YOUR LIFE A DIFFERENT PERSPECTIVE

For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut.
  • All. These. Benefits. Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top-of-the-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work.

Commitment & contract
Permanent or fixed-term. Full-time or part-time.

Location
Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland), or fully remote from eligible countries.

The selection process
In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. There are no preferential paths beyond what your application demonstrates, and we evaluate every candidate through the same process.

Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self-reject.

All applications go through our careers page, which is the only way to be considered. If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours. To learn more about what to expect throughout the selection process, you can find additional information here.

We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times.

We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form.

Before you apply
If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again.

Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment.

To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we expect you to spend most days in our Milan office during your first few months with us, regardless of your long-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from one of our offices, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage.

If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

Title: Data Scientist GenAI
Description:
Are you looking for a new challenge?

Fancy helping us shape the future of motor insurance?

Prima could be the place for you.

Since 2015, we’ve been using our love of data and tech to rethink motor insurance and bring drivers a great experience at a great price. Our story began in Italy, where we’ve quickly become the number one online motor insurance provider. In fact, we’re trusted by over 5 million drivers. And now we’re expanding to help millions more drivers in the UK and Spain.

We’re looking for a Data Scientist to join our AI team focused on bringing ML & Generative AI into the heart of our operations (e.g. claims, underwriting, customer support).

In this role, you’ll have the opportunity to redefine the core operative processes, leveraging ML & GenAI techniques to enhance not only efficiency but, most importantly, the overall effectiveness and quality of outcomes.

You’ll collaborate with data scientists, engineers, and business leaders to design and deliver AI-driven products that create tangible business value and redefine the future of ops at Prima.

Excited to make an impact? Here are the details

What You’ll Do


Leverage Machine Learning and GenAI to solve complex business problems, making our processes better and faster;
Analyze and interpret complex data to support decision-making;
Contribute directly to Prima’s long-term success and financial impact through concrete solutions.


What We’re Looking For


Strong academic background in quantitative fields such as Math, Physics, Data Science, or Engineering;
All experience levels, including recent graduates with no prior working experience;
Strong analytical and quantitative skills;
Structured problem solving skills and being able to analyze and break down complex problems;
Curiosity, proactivity, quick learning and an innovative mindset.


Nice-to-Have


Hands-on experience with Python and coding best practices;
Experience with machine learning and Generative AI (openAI/Gemini APIs, agentic frameworks, …)
Business proficiency in Italian or Spanish, in addition to English.


Why you’ll love it here

We want to make Prima a happy and empowering place to work. So if you decide to join us, you can expect plenty of perks..

🤸 Work Your Way: Enjoy full flexibility – work from home, the office or a mix of both. Plus, work from anywhere for up to 30 days a year.

🏁 Grow with us: We may move fast at Prima, but we move together. Get access to learning resources, mentorship and a growth plan tailored to you.

🌈 Thrive and perform: Your best work begins when you feel your best. Enjoy private healthcare, gym discounts, wellbeing programs and mental health support.

Think you’re a match? Apply now.

At Prima, we celebrate uniqueness. If you don’t meet every requirement but are passionate about this role, we still want to hear from you. Innovation thrives on diverse perspectives.

Prima is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Need accommodations during the process? Email us at accessible.recruiting@prima.it. Let’s build the future of insurance, together.

Title: Sr Data Scientist
Description:
The Company
PayPal has been revolutionizing commerce globally for more than 25 years. Creating innovative experiences that make moving money, selling, and shopping simple, personalized, and secure, PayPal empowers consumers and businesses in approximately 200 markets to join and thrive in the global economy.

We operate a global, two-sided network at scale that connects hundreds of millions of merchants and consumers. We help merchants and consumers connect, transact, and complete payments, whether they are online or in person. PayPal is more than a connection to third-party payment networks. We provide proprietary payment solutions accepted by merchants that enable the completion of payments on our platform on behalf of our customers.

We offer our customers the flexibility to use their accounts to purchase and receive payments for goods and services, as well as the ability to transfer and withdraw funds. We enable consumers to exchange funds more safely with merchants using a variety of funding sources, which may include a bank account, a PayPal or Venmo account balance, PayPal and Venmo branded credit products, a credit card, a debit card, certain cryptocurrencies, or other stored value products such as gift cards, and eligible credit card rewards. Our PayPal, Venmo, and Xoom products also make it safer and simpler for friends and family to transfer funds to each other. We offer merchants an end-to-end payments solution that provides authorization and settlement capabilities, as well as instant access to funds and payouts. We also help merchants connect with their customers, process exchanges and returns, and manage risk. We enable consumers to engage in cross-border shopping and merchants to extend their global reach while reducing the complexity and friction involved in enabling cross-border trade.

Our beliefs are the foundation for how we conduct business every day. We live each day guided by our core values of Inclusion, Innovation, Collaboration, and Wellness. Together, our values ensure that we work together as one global team with our customers at the center of everything we do – and they push us to ensure we take care of ourselves, each other, and our communities.

Job Summary:

We are seeking a Sr. Data Scientist to join the CRM and Media Marketing Analytics team, where they will lead analytical strategy and execution across customer relationship management, paid media, and marketing effectiveness throughout the consumer lifecycle. This is a high-impact individual contributor role that blends deep analytical expertise with compelling storytelling and the ability to influence cross-functional stakeholders.

You will become an expert in PayPal’s consumer funnel, uncovering insights into acquisition, engagement, and retention across products, markets, and customer segments. Your work will translate large and complex data sets into actionable recommendations that inform strategies and inspire confidence in senior leaders and cross-functional partners.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in both the technical rigor of data science and the art of storytelling through clear communication and visualization.

Job Description:

Essential Responsibilities:


Lead the development and implementation of advanced data science models.
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand requirements.
Drive best practices in data science.
Ensure data quality and integrity in all processes.
Mentor and guide junior data scientists.
Stay updated with the latest trends in data science.


Minimum Qualifications:


3+ years relevant experience and a Bachelor’s degree OR Any equivalent combination of education and experience.


Additional Responsibilities & Preferred Qualifications:


Bachelor’s or advanced degree in a quantitative field (Computer Science, Statistics, Engineering, Mathematics, Economics, or related).
6-8 years of experience in data science, analytics, or related roles, ideally within financial services, internet services, technology, or comparable data-rich complex industries.
Proficiency in SQL, Excel, and data visualization tools (Tableau, Looker). Strong programming skills in Python or R.
Experience applying statistical modeling, machine learning, or experimentation to marketing and media problems.
Ability to independently design, execute, and communicate analyses that influence decision-making.
Experience partnering with media agencies and mar-tech/ad-tech teams to enhance data quality, measurement, and targeting capabilities.
Intellectual curiosity, problem-solving drive, and comfort with ambiguity and complexity.
Exceptional communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to influence senior leaders and cross-functional teams.


Subsidiary:

PayPal

Travel Percent:

0

PayPal does not charge candidates any fees for courses, applications, resume reviews, interviews, background checks, or onboarding. When making an application directly, we will never ask you to share passwords, one-time passcodes (OTP), or verification codes. Any such request is a red flag and likely part of a scam. All communication regarding your application will come from official PayPal email domains. If you suspect fraudulent activity, please report it immediately. To learn more about how to identify and avoid recruitment fraud please visit https://careers.pypl.com/contact-us.

For the majority of employees, PayPal's balanced hybrid work model offers 3 days in the office for effective in-person collaboration and 2 days at your choice of either the PayPal office or your home workspace, ensuring that you equally have the benefits and conveniences of both locations.

Our Benefits:

At PayPal, we’re committed to building an equitable and inclusive global economy. And we can’t do this without our most important asset-you. That’s why we offer comprehensive, choice-based programs, to support all aspects of personal wellbeing—physical, emotional, and financial—delivering meaningful value where it matters most. We strive to create a flexible, balanced work culture with a holistic approach to benefits, including generous paid time off, healthcare coverage for you and your family, and resources to create financial security and support your mental health.

Who We Are:

Click Here to learn more about our culture and community.

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

PayPal provides equal employment opportunity (EEO) to all persons regardless of age, color, national origin, citizenship status, physical or mental disability, race, religion, creed, gender, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, genetic information, marital status, status with regard to public assistance, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. In addition, PayPal will provide reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. If you are unable to submit an application because of incompatible assistive technology or a disability, please contact us at paypalglobaltalentacquisition@paypal.com.

Belonging at PayPal:

Our employees are central to advancing our mission, and we strive to create an environment where everyone can do their best work with a sense of purpose and belonging. Belonging at PayPal means creating a workplace with a sense of acceptance and security where all employees feel included and valued. We are proud to have a diverse workforce reflective of the merchants, consumers, and communities that we serve, and we continue to take tangible actions to cultivate inclusivity and belonging at PayPal.

Any general requests for consideration of your skills, please Join our Talent Community.

We know the confidence gap and imposter syndrome can get in the way of meeting spectacular candidates. Please don’t hesitate to apply.

Title: Software Engineer AI
Description:
AGAP2 è un gruppo europeo di consulenza ingegneristica e operativa facente parte del Gruppo MoOngy.







Ruolo:

Stiamo ricercando una figura di Software Engineer con passione per l’AI, che collaborerà allo sviluppo di applicazioni software e web utilizzando tecnologie all’avanguardia. Sarà coinvolta in tutte le fasi del ciclo di sviluppo del software, lavorando in team multidisciplinari.




Responsabilità:

Sviluppare e mantenere applicazioni software e web utilizzando metodologie di programmazione orientata agli oggetti, programmazione funzionale e tecniche di Test-Driven Development (TDD).
Collaborare con team multidisciplinari per progettare soluzioni software innovative, integrando funzionalità di Intelligenza Artificiale tramite Large Language Models (LLM) e sistemi multi-agente.
Partecipare a tutte le fasi del ciclo di sviluppo software, dalla progettazione alla distribuzione, garantendo qualità, scalabilità e manutenzione del codice.
Gestire e ottimizzare l’infrastruttura cloud per supportare le applicazioni e i servizi AI, con un occhio all’efficienza e all’affidabilità.
Integrare flussi di lavoro e sistemi AI complessi, sfruttando framework come LangChain, LongGraph, Temporal, e tecnologie di database vettoriali.
Adottare best practice di sviluppo software, inclusi CI/CD, architetture pulite (Clean Architecture), Domain-Driven Design (DDD), e metodologie Agile.




Competenze richieste:

Sviluppo software e web app con REST, WebSocket, Microservizi, Event-based e Asynchronous Programming
Principi di Clean Architecture e Domain-Driven Design (DDD)
Conoscenza di OOP, programmazione funzionale e Test-Driven Development (TDD)
Esperienza con CI/CD e lavoro in ambienti Agile




Competenze preferenziali:

Linguaggi: Python e TypeScript
Framework: LangChain, LongGraph, FastAPI, Temporal, React, Tailwind, ShadCN
Database: SQL, NoSQL, vettoriali
Esperienza con AWS e principi di MLOps
Conoscenza della lingua italiana e inglese




Modalità: ibrida, 1 giorno in presenza a Milano







The world needs you!

AGAP2 è un equal opportunities employer: crediamo che tutte le forme di diversità siano fonte di arricchimento reciproco. Ci impegniamo a garantire un ambiente di lavoro inclusivo, nel quale ogni singola risorsa possa apportare un valore unico alla nostra realtà. Pertanto, accogliamo candidature senza distinzione di età, genere, nazionalità, disabilità, orientamento sessuale, religione o stato civile. Inoltre, teniamo fede ai nostri impegni prestando attenzione alle risorse appartenenti alle categorie protette ai sensi degli articoli 1 e 18 della L. 68/99.

I dati personali saranno trattati in conformità alle disposizioni contenute nel regolamento UE 2016/679.

Title: React Native Engineer
Description:
Akkodis è un leader globale nel mercato dell'ingegneria e della ricerca e sviluppo, che mira ad accelerare l'innovazione e la trasformazione digitale utilizzando la forza dei connected data.

Con la passione per la tecnologia e il talento, il gruppo, composto da più di 40.000 ingegneri ed esperti digitali e con un fatturato di €4bn, offre una profonda esperienza intersettoriale in 28 paesi tra Nord America, EMEA e APAC.




Akkodis vanta un’esperienza quarantennale e un forte know-how in settori tecnologici chiave come la mobilità, i servizi software e tecnologici, la robotica, i test, le simulazioni, la sicurezza dei dati, l'intelligenza artificiale e l'analisi dei dati.




La combinazione di competenze informatiche e ingegneristiche porta a un'offerta unica di soluzioni end-to-end, con quattro linee di servizio - Consulting, Solutions, Talents e Academy - per supportare i clienti nel ripensare ai loro processi di sviluppo prodotto e di business, migliorare la produttività, ridurre al minimo il time-to-market e dare forma a un domani più smart e sostenibile.




La divisione IT di Akkodis è dedicata all'ICT e alla Digital Transformation, composta da oltre 200 consulenti altamente specializzati presenti su scala nazionale. Offriamo una vasta gamma di servizi, unendo talento e tecnologia per ottimizzare i processi IT e promuovere l'innovazione. Tra i nostri servizi principali troviamo Digital Transformation, Cloud Technologies e Data Strategy, che comprendono attività come ad esempio Software Engineering, Migrazione al Cloud, Data Governance, Artificial Intelligence, etc.




Nell’ottica di un potenziamento della divisione, ricerchiamo una figura di:




React Native Engineer




Entra a far parte della nostra divisione Digital, un gruppo di 200 consulenti che operano in Italia, specializzati nel settore ICT e nella Digital Transformation. La nostra forza risiede nell'integrazione delle nostre competenze, che ci consente di avere una visione a 360° delle esigenze tecnologiche e innovative delle aziende.




Quale sarà il tuo contributo in Akkodis?




Per un contesto giovane, dinamico e internazionale, siamo alla ricerca di un* Frontend Developer con esperienza nello sviluppo di applicazioni web e mobile. La risorsa sarà coinvolta nelle attività di progettazione, implementazione e testing di soluzioni digitali innovative, collaborando con team multidisciplinari in un ambiente orientato alla qualità e al raggiungimento dei risultati.




Ti occuperai di:




Sviluppare e mantenere applicazioni web moderne utilizzando React.
Partecipare all'implementazione di applicazioni mobile basate su React Native.
Collaborare con designer, backend developer e stakeholder per definire e realizzare nuove funzionalità.
Garantire la qualità del codice attraverso attività di testing e debugging.
Ottimizzare le performance e l'esperienza utente delle applicazioni.
Contribuire all'analisi tecnica e alla definizione delle soluzioni applicative.
Gestire in autonomia le attività assegnate rispettando tempi e priorità.
Partecipare alle attività di code review e al miglioramento continuo dei processi di sviluppo.




Hai una Laurea in ambito Informatico o simili e almeno 2 anni di esperienza in sviluppo FrontEnd con Reactjs e React Native?




About you




Requisiti per iniziare il tuo percorso di crescita:

Akkodis vanta un’esperienza quarantennale e un forte know-how in settori tecnologici chiave come la mobilità, i servizi software e tecnologici, la robotica, i test, le simulazioni, la sicurezza dei dati, l'intelligenza artificiale e l'analisi dei dati.


La combinazione di competenze informatiche e ingegneristiche porta a un'offerta unica di soluzioni end-to-end, con quattro linee di servizio - Consulting, Solutions, Talents e Academy - per supportare i clienti nel ripensare ai loro processi di sviluppo prodotto e di business, migliorare la produttività, ridurre al minimo il time-to-market e dare forma a un domani più smart e sostenibile.


La divisione IT di Akkodis è dedicata all'ICT e alla Digital Transformation, composta da oltre 200 consulenti altamente specializzati presenti su scala nazionale. Offriamo una vasta gamma di servizi, unendo talento e tecnologia per ottimizzare i processi IT e promuovere l'innovazione. Tra i nostri servizi principali troviamo Digital Transformation, Cloud Technologies e Data Strategy, che comprendono attività come ad esempio Software Engineering, Migrazione al Cloud, Data Governance, Artificial Intelligence, etc.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Are you looking for a new challenge?

Fancy helping us shape the future of motor insurance?

Prima could be the place for you.

Since 2015, we’ve been using our love of data and tech to rethink motor insurance and bring drivers a great experience at a great price. Our story began in Italy, where we’ve quickly become the number one online motor insurance provider. In fact, we’re trusted by over 5 million drivers. And now we’re expanding to help millions more drivers in the UK and Spain.

To help fuel that growth, we need a Machine Learning Engineering to join our Engineering Team.

This team is the beating heart of Prima.

You’ll be joining over 350 engineers across software development, infrastructure, operations and security. Fueled by curiosity, experimentation and collaboration, you’ll help deliver scalable, impactful solutions that shape the future of insurance.

Excited to make an impact? Here are the details

What You Will Do


Defining the model development methodologies and best practices, following the whole life cycle with a central role in choosing the technological stack;
Supporting the productionalization and evolution of the Oracles, entities that through machine learning pilot the behavior of Prima's microservices;
Developing the tools (packages, microservices, pipelines...) that you deem fundamental for your and your team job;
Growing, reading, and experimenting to always be up to speed with emerging technology.


What We’re Looking For


A robust programming background, spotlighting your prowess in Python;
A familiarity with the lifecycle of ML model development, including past experience in taking them to production;
Proficiency in software development practices like TDD and BDD, ensuring our code is marked by quality, readability, and maintainability;
A wealth of experience in software architecture, encompassing an understanding of common design patterns. Your focus on Machine Learning, Microservices, and DDD sets you apart;Exceptional command of the English language.


Nice-to-Have


Alongside the essential prerequisites, we're excited to find experience in version control of data and artefacts (like DeltaLake, DVC, or MLFlow), designing and operating cloud-based systems, preferably within AWS or Databricks, familiarity with messaging platforms and proficiency in infrastructure as code (e.g. Terraform, CloudFormation, Pulumi), a grasp of K8s and service mesh concepts, and previous exposure to data orchestration tools.


Why You’ll Love It Here

We want to make Prima a happy and empowering place to work. So if you decide to join us, you can expect plenty of perks.

🤸 Work Your Way: Enjoy full flexibility – work from home, the office or a mix of both. Plus, work from anywhere for up to 30 days a year.

🏁 Grow with us: We may move fast at Prima, but we move together. Get access to learning resources, mentorship and a growth plan tailored to you.

🌈 Thrive and perform: Your best work begins when you feel your best. Enjoy private healthcare, gym discounts, wellbeing programs and mental health support.

Think you’re a match? Apply now.

At Prima, we celebrate uniqueness. If you don’t meet every requirement but are passionate about this role, we still want to hear from you. Innovation thrives on diverse perspectives.

Prima is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Need accommodations during the process? Email us at accessible.recruiting@prima.it. Let’s build the future of insurance, together.
