Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
I'm currently working with an innovative company at the forefront of the AgriTech sector, leveraging AI and machine learning to transform the future of farming and sustainable food production. They are looking to hire a Machine Learning Engineer to help build intelligent solutions that improve crop yields, optimise resource usage, and drive greater efficiency across agricultural operations.




This is an opportunity to apply cutting-edge machine learning techniques to real-world challenges, helping shape a more sustainable and data-driven farming industry.

The Role




As a Machine Learning Engineer, you'll play a key role in developing and deploying production-grade ML solutions that support precision agriculture and smart farming initiatives. You'll work alongside software engineers, data scientists, and domain experts to deliver scalable systems capable of processing large volumes of sensor, satellite, and operational data.




Responsibilities

Design, build, and deploy machine learning models into production environments.
Develop data pipelines capable of processing large-scale agricultural and environmental datasets.
Build predictive models to improve crop health, yield forecasting, irrigation optimisation, and resource management.
Work with structured and unstructured data from IoT devices, sensors, drones, and satellite imagery.
Collaborate with engineering and product teams to translate business challenges into AI-driven solutions.
Implement MLOps best practices, including model monitoring, retraining, and automated deployment.
Explore emerging technologies including Generative AI and advanced analytics to enhance farming operations.




What They're Looking For

Essential Experience

3+ years of commercial experience building machine learning solutions.
Strong Python development skills.
Experience with PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, or similar ML frameworks.
Strong understanding of feature engineering, model evaluation, and optimisation techniques.
Experience working with large datasets and SQL databases.
Familiarity with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP.
Experience with Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to work within cross-functional teams.

Desirable

Experience working with geospatial data, satellite imagery, computer vision, or IoT datasets.
Exposure to time-series forecasting and predictive analytics.
Experience with Generative AI, LLMs, or MLOps platforms such as MLflow or Kubeflow.
Knowledge of distributed computing frameworks such as Spark or Ray.




Why Join?

Agriculture is undergoing a technological revolution, and my client is leading that change. Their mission is to empower farmers with intelligent tools that increase productivity, reduce waste, and support more sustainable food production.

This is an opportunity to work on meaningful problems where your models will have a tangible impact—not only on business performance but on the future of global agriculture.




If you're a Machine Learning Engineer looking to apply AI to one of the world's most essential industries, I'd be keen to speak with you.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Data Scientist (ML & GenAI)- Remote Contract | Spain, Portugal or Sweden




We're hiring a Data Scientist with a strong ML background and a genuine interest in GenAI to join a global engineering team working on two really interesting internal products.




This is a mid-level role with a July start, running through to September with a high likelihood of extension beyond that. You'll be embedded across both projects, working alongside existing data scientists and contributing to real, production-grade solutions — not just keeping things at PoC stage.




The Projects




The first is a field service scheduling platform — a live, actively developed tool that manages when engineers need to go on-site for maintenance. There's a working live schedule already in place and the team are continuing to build and iterate on it.




The second is a GenAI-powered chatbot that's moving in a more ambitious direction — the team are rebuilding the UI and evolving the product significantly. It's earlier stage, which means more room to shape the direction, with some genuinely interesting GenAI concepts in play.




What You'll Be Doing




Supporting both projects as an embedded Data Scientist, working closely with the existing science team
Developing and deploying ML models including predictive, prescriptive, and generative AI solutions
Integrating LLMs and GenAI capabilities into analytical workflows and production systems
Building and maintaining ETL pipelines on AWS
Analysing complex datasets to surface patterns and insights that drive real decisions
Designing experiments, performing statistical analysis, and validating model performance
Collaborating with cross-functional teams to translate business requirements into working data science solutions
Building with production in mind — this team needs someone who can deploy and manage systems, not just experiment




What We're Looking For




Strong ML and data science fundamentals — statistical modelling, supervised/unsupervised learning, feature engineering, hypothesis testing
Hands-on GenAI experience — LLMs, prompt engineering, RAG concepts
Solid Python and SQL skills — these are non-negotiable
ETL pipeline experience, ideally on AWS
Some software engineering background — you're comfortable deploying things and managing systems in a production environment
Experience with deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow or PyTorch
Familiarity with MLOps best practices, Docker, Git, and Jupyter
Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to work with less day-to-day direction
Based in Spain, Portugal, or Sweden




Nice to Have




Experience with data visualisation tools — matplotlib, seaborn, plotly
Knowledge of knowledge graphs and data connectivity
Familiarity with AWS CDK or similar IaC tooling
Experience working within large enterprise or industrial environments




The Details




Fully remote — B2B contract
Start date: beginning of July
Initial contract through to September — high chance of extension
Based in Spain, Portugal, or Sweden

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
The Role

You'll be working as a Data Scientist within an innovative health-tech company where you will apply machine learning, statistical modelling, and advanced analytics to develop data-driven solutions that improve healthcare delivery and patient care.




Responsibilities

Develop and deploy predictive models for healthcare applications including patient risk stratification, disease progression prediction, hospital readmission analysis, clinical decision support, patient segmentation, and healthcare resource forecasting.
Design and implement feature engineering and data preprocessing workflows for clinical, operational, patient-generated, electronic health record (EHR), and time-series healthcare data.
Apply statistical techniques and machine learning algorithms to solve complex healthcare challenges, improve patient outcomes, and support evidence-based clinical and operational decision-making.
Build, validate, and evaluate predictive models using appropriate performance metrics, validation methodologies, and experimentation frameworks to ensure accuracy, reliability, and clinical relevance.




Your Profile

2–5+ years of experience in Data Science, Machine Learning, Advanced Analytics, or a related field.
Strong programming skills in Python and experience with data science libraries such as Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, XGBoost, or similar.
Experience developing predictive models and applying statistical analysis to real-world problems.
Strong understanding of data preparation, feature engineering, and model evaluation methodologies.

Title: Data Scientist - Agriculture & Nature Analytics
Description:
Who You'll Work With

Driving lasting impact and building long-term capabilities with our clients is not easy work. You are the kind of person who thrives in a high performance/high reward culture - doing hard things, picking yourself up when you stumble, and having the resilience to try another way forward.

In return for your drive, determination, and curiosity, we'll provide the resources, mentorship, and opportunities you need to become a stronger leader faster than you ever thought possible. Your colleagues—at all levels—will invest deeply in your development, just as much as they invest in delivering exceptional results for clients. Every day, you'll receive apprenticeship, coaching, and exposure that will accelerate your growth in ways you won’t find anywhere else.

When you join us, you will have:


Continuous learning: Our learning and apprenticeship culture, backed by structured programs, is all about helping you grow while creating an environment where feedback is clear, actionable, and focused on your development. The real magic happens when you take the input from others to heart and embrace the fast-paced learning experience, owning your journey.
A voice that matters: From day one, we value your ideas and contributions. You’ll make a tangible impact by offering innovative ideas and practical solutions, all while upholding our unwavering commitment to ethics and integrity. We not only encourage diverse perspectives, but they are critical in driving us toward the best possible outcomes.
Global community: With colleagues across 65+ countries and over 100 different nationalities, our firm’s diversity fuels creativity and helps us come up with the best solutions for our clients. Plus, you’ll have the opportunity to learn from exceptional colleagues with diverse backgrounds and experiences.
World-class benefits: On top of a competitive salary (based on your location, experience, and skills), we provide a comprehensive benefits package to enable holistic well-being for you and your family.


Your Impact

You’ll work with McKinsey’s agricultural and nature analytics team (ACRE) in Brussels, Belgium, part of our Agriculture Practice and Nature & Natural Capital Service Line.

Our Agriculture Practice advises agribusiness, consumer food, government, and investor clients on strategic, marketing & sales, and operations issues, helping support industry-shaping decisions that impact the future of global food production.

The Nature & Natural Capital Service Line helps corporations, public-sector actors and institutions in enabling nature-positive outcomes by integrating biodiversity, land use, water, and ecosystem information into strategy, investment decisions, and operations.

Within this group, ACRE is a team of ~30 expert consultants, data scientists, and engineers focused on bringing cutting-edge analytics to our clients. We apply real-time data and advanced analytics or AI – generally predictive or geospatial in nature – to solve the biggest problems currently facing global agriculture and nature.

You will leverage industry knowledge and analytical expertise to provide insights both to clients as part of client service teams and within the ACRE team by strengthening the core products and algorithms we build for clients. You can expect to split your time delivering impact at clients (either remotely or on site) and building up ACRE’s core analytical offerings.

In the agriculture sector, issues to solve could include determining the suitability of growing different types of crops across different geographies, using local agronomic data and satellite imagery to determine market potential for input products, or optimizing farm operations using yield forecasting. For the nature sector, you may help public-sector clients from low- and middle-income clients define their nature conservation strategy, supporting them through geospatial analysis and your domain knowledge.

Along the way, you will receive best-in-class training in structuring business problems and serving as a client adviser, develop tech-skills through (in-person) trainings curated by big-tech companies, and have opportunities to work closely with and learn from our senior agriculture and nature leaders and industry players that are shaping the future of these fields. You will get access to unparalleled career acceleration, with a huge amount of ownership and responsibility from the get-go in a collaborative, diverse, non-hierarchical environment. You will get the opportunity to travel to client sites and attend in-person trainings, locally and around the world. Lastly, you will be able to provide direct and measurable impact towards solving some of the largest problems facing the global food-system and natural ecosystems.

Your Qualifications and Skills


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a quantitative and/or environmental-related discipline, such as agriculture, agronomy, biology, ecology, geography, climate, or related
Strong experience with agronomy/crop science, animal sciences and farm-level production systems, AND/OR nature- and natural-capital–related topics, such as biodiversity, land use, water systems, ecosystem services, or nature-related risk and impact assessment, preferred
Geospatial and spatiotemporal analysis, remote sensing, image processing, preferred
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, paired with the ability to develop creative and efficient solutions
Clean, efficient coding abilities in Python ideally with experience parallel processing and using cloud computing resources
Ability to design and develop geospatial workflows and pipelines for data analysis and processing; ability to use version-control & collaboration tools
Strong multitasking and parallel development abilities
Strong interpersonal communication skills
Creative, naturally curious, and willing to take intellectual risks
Able to work under competing, quickly-changing priorities, manage expectations effectively and support the team under pressure
Self-management skills and ability to work as part of an Agile team preferred
Willingness to travel up to 50%

Title: Data Scientist – Machine Learning & AI Job ID: JP054484
Description:
About the job Data Scientist – Machine Learning & AI Job ID: JP054484

Data Scientist – Machine Learning & AI

We are seeking a Data Scientist to design, develop, and maintain a modular and future-proof machine learning framework while delivering advanced analytics and predictive modeling solutions to support business objectives.

Key Responsibilities Machine Learning Framework Development


Design and implement a scalable and reusable machine learning framework.
Establish best practices for data preprocessing, model training, validation, deployment, and lifecycle management.
Implement monitoring, feedback loops, and model retraining mechanisms.
Manage model versioning, reproducibility, logging, and system monitoring.
Prepare infrastructure for future cloud migration initiatives.
Document processes, methodologies, and technical solutions.
Coach and support team members in the adoption and use of the framework.
Present technical analyses and results to business stakeholders.


Data Science & Machine Learning Solutions


Translate business requirements into technical solutions using an iterative development approach.
Identify, assess, and validate relevant data sources.
Perform data analysis, data preparation, and exploratory data analysis (EDA).
Develop feature engineering strategies to improve model performance.
Select, evaluate, compare, and optimize machine learning algorithms.
Build, tune, automate, and maintain predictive models.
Continuously evaluate and improve model accuracy and effectiveness.
Communicate insights and recommendations through clear visualizations and presentations.


Required Skills & Experience


Strong experience with Python for Data Science and Machine Learning.
Solid knowledge of SQL and database technologies.
Experience with Machine Learning and AI techniques for data analysis.
Experience developing and evaluating analytical and predictive models.
Hands-on experience with Jupyter Notebook and KNIME.
Knowledge of Microsoft Fabric.
Experience with Microsoft DevOps or similar CI/CD and version control platforms.
Experience working with Microsoft SQL databases.
Knowledge of Oracle database environments.
Familiarity with Azure Machine Learning and Azure ML SDK.


Languages


Native proficiency in either Dutch or French.
Ability to read and understand the other national language.


Working Model


Hybrid work environment.
On-site presence required 2 days per week in Brussels, with remote work available on remaining days.


Selection Process


Interview combined with a practical technical assignment.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Machine Learning Engineer




📍 Lokeren, Belgium | Full-Time | Biotech Startup




We are working with an innovative biotech startup based in Lokeren that is looking to hire a talented Machine Learning Engineer to join their growing technical team.

This is an exciting opportunity to work at the intersection of biotechnology, Machine Learning and AI, and software engineering, helping transform complex client data into scalable machine learning solutions that directly power cutting-edge software products.




You will collaborate closely with a small, highly skilled team with expertise across software development, Python engineering, and artificial intelligence.




The Role

As a Machine Learning Engineer, you will play a key role in designing and developing machine learning pipelines that convert client data into meaningful, production-ready outputs. You will be responsible for building robust data workflows, training models, and helping integrate intelligent systems into customer-facing products.




This role requires both strong technical capability and excellent communication skills, as you will work closely with both technical colleagues and external stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into effective ML solutions.




Responsibilities

Design and build scalable machine learning pipelines
Translate complex client datasets into actionable machine learning workflows
Develop and maintain data extraction, transformation, and pre-processing systems
Train, evaluate, and optimise machine learning models
Work with Python-based AI and software systems in a collaborative engineering environment
Support the integration of ML models into production software products
Collaborate with software engineers and technical stakeholders across projects
Communicate technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences




Skills & Experience

Experience with Python
Experience building machine learning models and production pipelines
Hands-on experience with either PyTorch or TensorFlow
Knowledge of data extraction, cleaning, and transformation processes
Understanding of ML lifecycle management and deployment practices
Ability to work independently within a small, agile team
Strong communication and collaboration skills
Passion for AI, machine learning, and innovative technology




Desirable Experience

Experience working with scientific, healthcare, or biotech data
Familiarity with cloud infrastructure or MLOps practices
Exposure to software product development environments




What’s on Offer

Opportunity to join an ambitious biotech startup at an early growth stage
High-impact role with real ownership and influence
Collaborative and technically driven team culture
Work on innovative AI-powered software products
Up to €5k gross - Dependent on ability and experience
Company car + Fuel Card or Mobility Budget available
Meal Vouchers
Hospitalisation Insurance
Group Insurance
Pension Plan
Hybrid working opportunities available




If you are passionate about applying machine learning to real-world biotech challenges and enjoy working in a fast-moving startup environment, we would love to hear from you.

Title: Software Engineer
Description:
The Analyst Developer is responsible for creating, testing and implementing IT programs and to guarantee the quality, user-friendliness and performance of the integrated systems. He/she plays a reference role in the development environment to deliver high-quality systems that meet the known and predefined standards within the agreed schedule.




Main responsibilities

System Development

Writing, improving, and testing programs based on detailed analyses; carrying out integration, regression, and performance tests to check how the applications’ components work together and interact.




Technical design

Translate the functional analysis into a technical analysis and provide documentation. Being capable of directly discussing with Business to capture their requirements and translate them into clear specifications for the development




Testing

Supporting the IT team in setting up an acceptance test environment and helping users run those tests; critically analyzing the results and making (or having) the necessary adjustments.

Implementation

Implement and follow up the P&V coding standards (programs, parameterization, documentation, ...) taking into account interactions with other domains and after approval of the business; coordinate with other domains during development activities







Main skills

Language
Mother tongue Dutch or French, with good knowledge of the other language
Good knowledge of English is a must




Must have
Bachelor’s degree in computer science or equivalent through experience
First experience in a similar position
Expert in Mulesoft (development, anypoint studio = must, integration analyse or architect experience = must)
Experience in preparing technical analysis
Knowledge of test methodology
Experience with MUnit
Experience with Salesforce (at least integration perspective)
Knowledge of API-Led Connectivity
Experience in development of event-driven architectures
Experience with SQL
Experience in making technical analysis
Experience in making and finetuning a canonical data model




Nice to have
Experience with Jira
Experience with Agile and scrum methodology
Experience in the Insurance sector




Soft skills
Communicative
Autonomous
Proactive (searching for solutions)
Flexible
Team spirit

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
The Role

You will be working within an innovative energy technology company that builds intelligent AI-driven solutions with the aim of improving energy efficiency, grid performance, and operational reliability. They are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to develop and deploy scalable ML solutions for real-world energy and infrastructure challenges.




What You’ll Be Doing

Develop and deploy machine learning models for energy-focused applications such as load forecasting, predictive maintenance, energy optimization, smart grid analytics, anomaly detection, and consumption forecasting
Create scalable ML infrastructure and pipelines for training, evaluation, monitoring, and real-time model serving across energy and industrial platforms
Design data preprocessing and feature engineering workflows for complex datasets including smart meter, IoT, sensor, geospatial, and time-series data
Help build intelligent monitoring and optimization systems leveraging technologies such as connected infrastructure, edge computing, renewable energy systems, and live sensor networks
Contribute to data-driven solutions that improve operational efficiency, optimize energy usage, reduce costs, and support the transition toward sustainable energy systems




What They’re Looking For

2–6+ years of experience in Machine Learning Engineering or similar AI-focused roles
Strong proficiency in Python
Hands-on experience with machine learning frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, or Scikit-learn
Track record of building, deploying, and maintaining ML systems in production
Experience working with energy, utilities, industrial, IoT, mobility, or time-series datasets is considered a strong plus
Understanding of data engineering practices including ETL/ELT pipelines, streaming systems, and large-scale data processing
Experience with cloud infrastructure such as AWS, Azure, or GCP
Interest in applying AI to energy systems, smart infrastructure, renewable technologies, or operational optimization challenges




What’s on Offer

Hybrid working model
Opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge energy technology solutions with tangible real-world impact
Collaborative environment with a strong emphasis on innovation, experimentation, and applied AI
Competitive salary package up to €6,000/month depending on experience
Company car or flexible mobility budget
13+ additional ADV leave days
Clear career progression opportunities toward senior and leadership roles
Mission-driven culture focused on building technology that supports the future of energy and sustainability




If this opportunity excites you, apply today or send your CV and a short cover letter to ryan.martin@vividresourcing.com.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Job Description:




Pre-Sales & Discovery

Partner with the Sales Team on client discovery calls to provide technical depth, assess solution fit, and scope Data-as-a-Service opportunities
Develop and present tailored technical assets including specifications, data dictionaries, sample datasets, and client-specific demonstrations to illustrate feasibility and value
Define project scope and success criteria in collaboration with customer stakeholders and internal delivery teams, ensuring alignment on technical requirements and capacity
Design and execute calibration processes including baseline batches, benchmark reports, and evaluation frameworks that establish measurable project success metrics

Project Execution & Delivery

Build and deploy evaluators, design and implement quality measurement systems to validate project outputs and ensure deliverables meet client expectations
Generate synthetic datasets by developing or adapting existing pipelines to accelerate client engagements and augment training data
Package and deliver production-grade datasets with standardized formatting, comprehensive documentation, and quality assurance
Configure and build custom applications and off-platform solutions for non-standard or specialized client requirements




Production & Technical Partnership

Define production specifications and workflows, securing technical alignment with client teams to enable seamless go-live transitions
Provide ongoing technical support to Delivery Managers, addressing complex questions, resolving technical blockers, and supporting customer rebuttals
Maintain specification consistency and alignment across customer and internal teams throughout the engagement lifecycle
Identify and document workflow best practices and automation opportunities, collaborating with DaaS Engineering to continuously improve delivery capabilities




Technical Leadership & Innovation

Maintain solution leaderboards and execute custom model benchmarking on existing datasets to demonstrate technical capabilities

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Sia is a next-generation, global management consulting group. Founded in 1999, we were born digital. Today our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change and we help clients initiate, navigate and benefit from transformation. We believe optimism is a force multiplier, helping clients to mitigate downside and maximize opportunity. With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results. Our optimism transforms outcomes.





Job Description



What you will do

As an expert in the field of Machine Learning Engineering you will be responsible for the growth of our Data team, both professionally as well as in size. You will be in charge of shaping and executing the future of your expertise within Sia. You will work on client projects as well as on building our own Sia AI solutions, together with our Dutch team of Data Scientists, our French Data Engineering team, and other business units of Sia Global.



Your responsibilities include:



Leading an internal team towards the development of AI products;
Working in a multidisciplinary team on client projects as an expert on your subject;
Making architectural and design decisions for cloud environments;
Building production level ML/AI solutions with solid software engineering;
Guiding Data Scientists to scale up their models into production (parallelizing, optimizing, tuning, testing, and wrapping);
Giving trainings, leading internal meetings and organizing external events;
Participating in team meetings and workshops with clients;
Writing blog articles, presenting at conferences and sending out press releases on the subject that you lead.

Who you will work with

The Data Science team is young, diverse and growing (10+). You will join a team that will challenge you and invest in your professional development. We have a flat structure and rely on the entrepreneurial spirit of our members: initiative is highly encouraged. We are striving for well-being, diversity and knowledge-sharing inside the team, so that we can grow together.



Who you are



You are an established technical professional that wants to make the next step in their career, taking on more leadership responsibilities.
You have first-rate project management and time management skills.
You are a true problem solver, you turn complex issues into simple and impactful business solutions.
You are enthusiastic for applying Data Science/Engineering solutions to business problems.
You have excellent communication skills and can present complicated concepts in a synthetic way.
You have the ability to adapt rapidly and work on a range of different business areas within a challenging environment.
You have the ability to work collaboratively in a team environment.

In return, you can expect:



An international company that gives importance to diversity, inclusion, employee well-being and corporate social responsibilities actions.
A great working atmosphere with friendly and professional colleagues where innovation and entrepreneurship are encouraged
A personal development plan with a regular follow-up as well training opportunities to develop yourself
A competitive salary with great advantages, like mobility budget, hospital insurance, performance -related bonus,...


Qualifications


MSc level education in Computer Science, Applied Mathematics, Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems or related field of study.
2-4 years of experience in building production-quality ML/Deep Learning pipelines and models.
Good understanding of Machine Learning fundamentals and strong knowledge in a specific domain (e.g., Computer Vision, Deep Learning, NLP).
Expert coding skills in Python and at least one of these languages: Scala, C++, Java.
Hands-on experience with database management languages (e.g., SQL, PostgreSQL).
Hands-on experience in cloud-based infrastructures (AWS/GCP/Azure).
Hands-on experience with Unix-based command line and DevOps tools (Git, Docker, Kubernetes).
Hands-on experience with big data technologies (e.g. Spark, Hadoop, Databricks).
You have a strong interest in data and AI-related topics, the ability to quickly build expertise in these areas, and you’re excited about helping our clients seize the opportunities they offer.
Experience with coaching/mentoring other engineers.
Prior experience in Management Consulting is a strong plus.
Willingness to travel and work at local and international clients.
Fluency in spoken and written English, French and Dutch is required.


Additional Information



Unfortunately, we are unable to provide a work permit for this position.



Sia is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or discipline, are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, or business needs.

Title: Data Scientist - Waterloo, Belgium (F/M)
Description:
What we will accomplish together:

Alsinova is looking on a regular basis for Data Scientist Consultants to join our consulting team for clients' projects (from big pharma to small biotech) based in Belgium.

Your mission (should you decide to accept it):

Your potential challenges will be various, depending on the clients' priorities. The list below gives you examples of our classic activities.

Your daily activities will be:


Use of advanced analytics technologies, including machine learning and predictive modeling.
Gathering and analyzing data, using various types of analytics and reporting tools to detect patterns, trends and relationships in data sets.
Use statistical approaches to solve analytical problems.
Develop custom systems (hardware and software) internally and with external partners.
Work in various projects/therapeutic areas: neurosciences, oncology, in vivo and in vitro research activities, genomics, patient follow-ups, safety and risk management, etc.
Work in close interaction with stakeholders to install and implement specific equipment, tools and systems to support.
Provide documentation and procedures to other stakeholders.
Provide activities planning.
Project management and cost reporting.


You:


Engineering or biologist degree, data scientist.
At least 5 years of experience as a data scientist, or computer scientist, or database and software programmer. Knowledge of :
Microsoft office.
Programming languages (C, MATLAB, R, Python) and visualization tools (PowerBI, Tableau)
Signal processing.
Statistics and algorithms.
AI, machine learning, deep learning knowledge, big data.
Database knowledge (SQL, Oracle).
English and French fluent.


Our Benefits

Joining Astek means choosing:


an experience accelerator, where every assignment is an opportunity to progress
access to a multitude of technically exciting projects for our clients as well as internally
individualized, local support for a tailored career path
continuous learning, thanks to our internal training academy
a friendly and inclusive work environment, supported by #TopEmployer2026 certified HR practices Let's move forward.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
ML OPS Engineer / AI Engineer

About NORRIQ

NORRIQ is an international group based in Denmark and Belgium, comprising over 350 consultants dedicated to unlocking the full business potential of companies through technology and digital transformation.

We have a specialized Financial Services practice that is experiencing rapid growth. Over 100 consultants focus exclusively on the financial services sector, assisting banks and insurances in redesigning their operations to meet changing client expectations, adopt new technologies, and adhere to evolving regulations (DORA, RIS, AI Act, AML, KYC, etc).

At NORRIQ, our employees are at the heart of our organization and our success.. We hire and nurture passionate individuals who strive for excellence for both our clients and colleagues. Our culture supports personal and professional growth with training, opportunities, and creative freedom. Regardless of when you join or how long you stay, you'll experience an unforgettable NORRIQ journey and become a part of our extended family. Connected forever!

Role Overview

As an MLOps Engineer / AI Engineer, you will play a central role in bridging Data Science and IT Operations. You will design, deploy, and scale production-grade machine learning and AI solutions for our financial services clients. Your mission is to ensure robust, secure, and efficient AI operations—covering deployment, observability, lifecycle management, and continuous improvement.

This role is ideal for someone who loves building AI infrastructure, optimizing performance, and enabling data scientists to move faster and safer.

Key Responsibilities


Deploy ML/AI Models: Work with Data Scientists to deploy secure, scalable, and reliable models into production.
Build CI/CD Pipelines: Automate testing, packaging, and deployment for data and model workflows.
Manage Data Pipelines: Design and maintain efficient data flows across storage systems, databases, and platforms.
Set Up Environments: Manage dependencies, development environments, and tooling for smooth project onboarding.
Operate Kubernetes: Deploy and maintain containerized AI components using Kubernetes.
Monitor & Troubleshoot: Oversee model/application performance, resolve incidents, and conduct root-cause analyses.
Optimize & Innovate: Identify improvements, adopt new technologies, and enhance AI engineering practices.


Requirements

Education


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Strong background in programming, system engineering, or DevOps is highly valued.


Technical Skills


Strong proficiency in Python.
Experience with REST APIs and Git.
Hands-on expertise in CI/CD and MLOps/DevOps practices.
Practical experience with Docker and Kubernetes.
Familiarity with Linux, software testing, and modern software engineering workflows.
Nice to have: SQL/PostgreSQL, MLflow, Helm, or related tooling.


Soft Skills


Collaborative and team-oriented mindset.
Proactive, solution-driven, and comfortable working in dynamic environments.
Strong communication, analytical, and problem-solving abilities.


Languages


Fluent in English (spoken and written).
Knowledge of additional languages (Dutch or French) is a plus.


Why Joining Us?


You will be part of a fast growing international company with a unique people culture
A very competitive salary package including a company car, iPhone and different allowances
You will get extensive trainings in Belgium and Denmark on technical matters, as well as soft skills and project management, and you will have access to new technologies and innovative equipment.
You will join a dynamic and growing team with a great mix of young and experienced professionals focusing on financial services.

Title: Academic Researcher - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Academic Researcher

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input.

Key Responsibilities


Conduct in-depth research and analysis in your area of expertise, delivering high-quality, evidence-based insights.
Prepare, format, and review complex documents in Word and PDF, utilizing advanced features such as tracked changes, form filling, and data extraction.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including legal, healthcare, policy, and finance professionals, to support diverse project needs.
Translate complex research findings into clear, concise reports and presentations tailored for Fortune 500 standards.
Evaluate and synthesize academic literature, legal documents, or industry data to inform project outcomes.
Ensure accuracy, consistency, and compliance with designated guidelines in all written deliverables.
Maintain clear and professional written and verbal communication with project stakeholders and team members.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Minimum 3+ years of relevant professional experience in academic research, legal, policy, healthcare, or related sectors.
Master’s degree, PhD, or JD from a recognized institution.
Demonstrated expertise in preparing and reviewing Word and PDF documents (tracked changes, formatting, data extraction, document review).
Outstanding written and verbal communication skills; meticulous attention to detail.
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a remote, deadline-driven environment.
Experience collaborating with multidisciplinary teams on complex deliverables.
Proven record of producing high-quality, client-ready outputs.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience working with or for Fortune 500 companies.
Prior involvement in legal, healthcare, policy, or financial operations document workflows.
Active publication or presentation record in academic or industry settings.

Title: Senior Python Data Scraping Engineer (Freelance)
Description:
Mindrift is looking for highly skilled Senior Python Data Scraping Engineers to join the Tendem project and drive specialized data scraping workflows within our hybrid AI + human system.

In this role, as an AI Pilot - that's how we refer to this role at Mindrift - you'll collaborate with Tendem Agents that handle repetitive tasks, while you provide critical thinking, domain expertise, and quality control to deliver accurate and actionable results.

This part-time remote opportunity is ideal for technical professionals with hands-on experience in web scraping, data extraction and processing.

What We Do

The Mindrift platform connects specialists with AI projects from major tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the potential of Generative AI by tapping into real-world expertise from across the globe.

This is a freelance role for a Tendem project. As a Senior Python Data Scraping Engineer, you'll handle data scraping tasks requiring technical precision for web extraction and processing, utilizing various tools such as our provided Apify and OpenRouter alongside your own resourceful approaches.

Key Responsibilities:


Own end-to-end data extraction workflows across complex websites, ensuring complete coverage, accuracy, and reliable delivery of structured datasets
Leverage internal tools (Apify, OpenRouter) alongside custom workflows to accelerate data collection, validation, and task execution while meeting defined requirements
Ensure reliable extraction from dynamic and interactive web sources, adapting approaches as needed to handle JavaScript-rendered content and changing site behavior
Enforce data quality standards through validation checks, cross-source consistency controls, adherence to formatting specifications, and systematic verification prior to delivery
Scale scraping operations for large datasets using efficient batching or parallelization, monitor failures, and maintain stability against minor site structure changes


Requirements:


At least 5+ years of relevant experience in data engineering, web scraping, automation, or software development (required)
Bachelor's or Master's Degree in Engineering, Applied Mathematics, Computer Science, or related technical fields is a plus
Candidates should have a strong technical foundation and practical experience with scripting, automation, and AI-assisted workflows. We are looking for specialists who can solve non-trivial problems, work confidently with LLMs, and systematically collect, structure, and validate data from diverse sources. A methodical, detail-oriented approach and the ability to work independently are essential
Strong experience in Python web scraping (BeautifulSoup, Selenium or similar), including dynamic content (JS, AJAX, infinite scroll) and APIs via proxies
Proven ability to extract data from complex structures (hierarchies, archived pages, inconsistent HTML)
Solid background in data cleaning, normalization, and validation, delivering structured datasets (CSV, JSON, Google Sheets)
Demonstrated experience handling anti-bot mechanisms and dynamic site structures at scale
Experience with cloud infrastructure (AWS or equivalent) and containerization (Docker) as part of real workflows
Hands-on experience with LLM frameworks (LangChain, OpenRouter, or similar) applied to automation tasks
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy
Self-directed work ethic with ability to troubleshoot independently
A link to GitHub is a plus
English proficiency: Upper-intermediate (B2) or above (required)


Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $45 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

  • Demonstrated experience handling anti-bot mechanisms and dynamic site structures at scale
  • Experience with cloud infrastructure (AWS or equivalent) and containerization (Docker) as part of real workflows
  • Hands-on experience with LLM frameworks (LangChain, OpenRouter, or similar) applied to automation tasks
  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy
  • Self-directed work ethic with ability to troubleshoot independently
  • A link to GitHub is a plus
  • English proficiency: Upper-intermediate (B2) or above (required)

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $45 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Data Scientist H/F
Description:
MCA BELGIUM, filiale du groupe français MCA, est une société de conseil en ingénierie et hautes technologies. Avec plus de 15 ans d’expérience en Belgique, nous accompagnons nos clients dans la réalisation de projets industriels complexes dans divers secteurs d’activité (énergie, nucléaire, pharmaceutique, aéronautique, ferroviaire).

Nous rejoindre, c’est intégrer une entreprise dynamique qui place l’accompagnement de ses collaborateurs au cœur de ses priorités. Vous bénéficierez d’un suivi personnalisé et de nombreuses formations pour développer vos compétences.

Votre Rôle

Rattaché(e) au pôle Data & Engineering, vous intervenez sur l’exploitation avancée de données industrielles issues d’équipements d’énergies renouvelables (solaire & éolien), avec pour objectif d’optimiser la performance des installations et d’anticiper les défaillances :


Analyse de données provenant de machines industrielles (production, capteurs, historiques de fonctionnement)
Conception et développement de modèles statistiques et algorithmiques (machine learning, détection d’anomalies)
Identification des pertes de performance, anomalies et risques de panne
Participation à la mise en place de solutions de maintenance prédictive
Contribution à l’optimisation du potentiel des équipements
Restitution des résultats via rapports techniques et documentation associée
Collaboration avec les équipes multidisciplinaires (ingénierie, experts terrain, métiers)
Veille sur les évolutions technologiques et méthodologiques en data science appliquée à l’industrie


Votre Profil


Diplôme d’ingénieur ou formation équivalente, idéalement avec une spécialisation en data science, mathématiques appliquées ou domaine proche
Première expérience professionnelle en data (stage long, VIE ou premier poste accepté), avec exposition concrète à des projets data
Maîtrise de Python
Bonne compréhension des algorithmes, statistiques et approches machine learning
Esprit analytique, rigueur et sens du travail en équipe
Maîtrise du français & anglais professionnel


Vos Perspectives D’évolution

Nous souhaitons vous accompagner dans votre montée en compétences. Notre objectif est de vous donner toutes les clés pour réussir et accéder aux différentes opportunités d’évolution. Vous pourrez évoluer vers des fonctions techniques, de gestion de projet ou de management. Ce poste est ouvert aux personnes en situation de handicap.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
We are seeking a driven and creative Data Scientist with a minimum of 3 years of experience in a data science or machine learning role, who will be instrumental in building predictive models, testing hypotheses, and providing statistically sound insights that enhance our lead generation capabilities. The Data Scientist will be part of our analytics team, working closely with IT, marketing, media buying and CRO teams to transform raw data into actionable insights, whether that means building real-time scoring models, investigating campaign performance, or helping the business distinguish signal from noise when making decisions.

The ideal candidate is someone who thrives on solving ambiguous problems, brings strong statistical intuition alongside machine learning skills, and can serve as a trusted analytical partner to stakeholders across the business. They're as comfortable building production ML pipelines as they are answering a media buyer's question about whether a source is actually underperforming or just experiencing variance. Experience in digital marketing, lead generation, or ad-tech is highly desirable.

Primary Responsibilities


Leverage agentic AI-assisted development tools throughout the workflow, from exploratory analysis and feature engineering to model development and pipeline building, to accelerate iteration speed while maintaining rigor and code quality
Design, develop, and deploy machine learning models to segment and score landing page visitors based on intent and lead quality, using behavioral signals such as scroll depth, click patterns, session duration, and form engagement.
Engineer predictive features from raw user behavior data to improve model accuracy and business relevance.
Build and maintain real-time scoring pipelines that enable dynamic postback signals to ad networks, improving campaign optimization speed and efficiency.
Collaborate with marketing and analytics teams to define quality/intent tiers and translate business logic into quantifiable model outputs.
Conduct exploratory data analysis and experimentation to uncover patterns in user behavior that correlate with downstream lead quality and conversion outcomes.
Continuously monitor, evaluate, and iterate on model performance, ensuring alignment with evolving business goals and traffic patterns.
Contribute to forecasting and budget projection initiatives as needed, supporting strategic planning with data-driven modeling.
Clearly communicate, document and present findings, methodologies, and model specifications for cross-functional, non-technical stakeholders.
Support data-driven decision-making by quantifying uncertainty, assessing statistical significance, and challenging assumptions when the data doesn't support them.
Identify and quantify relationships between behavioral signals, traffic sources, campaign attributes, and lead quality outcomes; distinguish meaningful correlations from spurious ones.
Serve as an analytical partner to media buyers and marketing teams: investigate campaign and source performance, validate hypotheses about lead quality, and provide statistically rigorous answers to business questions.


Requirements:


3+ years of experience in data science, machine learning, or a related quantitative role.
Comfortable working in agentic AI-assisted development environments (e.g., Cursor, ClaudeCode, Antigravity) to accelerate model development, data exploration, and pipeline building, including the judgment to know when AI-generated outputs need manual verification or refinement.
Strong proficiency in Python, including ML libraries such as scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, or similar.
Solid experience with feature engineering, particularly from behavioral, clickstream, or event-based data.
Familiarity with deploying models in production environments; experience with real-time or near-real-time inference is a strong plus.
Strong foundation in statistical inference, hypothesis testing and root-cause analysis: can distinguish signal from noise, assess significance, and communicate uncertainty clearly.
Proficiency in SQL and experience working with large-scale datasets.
Understanding of classification, regression, and clustering techniques; experience with user segmentation or propensity modeling preferred.
Exposure to digital marketing concepts and KPIs (CPL, CVR, ROAS, etc.) is highly desirable.
Ability to translate vague business questions into testable hypotheses and structured analyses.
Excellent problem-solving skills with the ability to work through ambiguity and define structure where none exists.
Strong communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Team-oriented mindset with a collaborative and proactive approach.
Upper-Intermediate or higher level of English.


Nice to Have


Experience building or using custom skills, prompt workflows, or tool-use patterns within agentic coding environments.
Experience in lead generation, ad-tech, or performance marketing environments.
Familiarity with ad network optimization, postback mechanisms, or conversion APIs.
Experience with cloud platforms and MLOps tools.
Background in time-series forecasting or demand modeling.


What We Offer


Remote Work - we offer a truly and fully remote environment. You choose where you are the most productive and comfortable to have an impact.
Paid vacations - generous paid vacation policy to ensure you have time to recharge
Unlimited Sick Days - we understand that being only human means getting sick or feeling under the weather from time to time, so we guarantee you time off as long as you need to recover and get back on your feet.
Ongoing Learning - people at CapsLock are deeply inquisitive and eager to learn new knowledge and skills, that's why we support and create learning opportunities like the Free Books program, workshops, conferences, and more.
Home Office - we will cover the equipment and furniture expenses to make sure you have the best work-from-home experience.
Physical Well–Being - We will cover costs of your medical insurance. Additionally we offer an allowance for a flexible fitness program.
Fun Stuff - there is never a shortage of fun stuff

Title: MLOps Engineer
Description:
1 - Working at the VUB

Are you our MLOps Engineer who will help us to get to the next level with ML/AI?

VUB is always looking for new talent to grow with us.

For more than 50 years, the Vrije Universiteit Brussel has stood for freedom, equality and solidarity, and this is very much alive on our campuses among students and staff alike.

At the VUB, you will find a diverse collection of personalities: innovators pur sang, but above all people who are 100% their authentic selves. With some 4,000 employees, we are the largest Dutch-speaking employer, in the private sector, in Brussels; an international city with which we are only too happy to connect and where (around) our 4 campuses are located.

Add to this our principle of free research - in which self-reflection, a critical attitude and an open, creative mind around scientific and social issues are central - and you have a university that is fundamentally groundbreaking and pioneering in education and research. In short: the VUB all over again.

Moreover, the VUB is a member of EUTOPIA, an alliance of like-minded European universities, all ready to reinvent themselves

2 - Position Description

The Scientific Compute & Data team ( https://hpc.vub.be ) provides scientific computing and data storage services to the research community. Amongst others the team operates sofia, the new Tier-1 supercomputer of the VSC (Flemish Supercomputer Centre, https://www.vscentrum.be ), the VUB Tier-2 supercomputer, and a large-capacity storage platform. The team also contributes to the Belgian AI Factory Antenna , which offers AI/ML services to companies and academia along with access to the AI-optimized hardware of the connected AI Factories ( LUMI-AI and JAIF ).

At the same time, the VUB is exploring the use of AI in its internal processes. Together with the VUB's AI researchers, several new GenAI-based pilot applications are being developed. These applications now need to be deployed and maintained in a reliable, secure, and scalable production environment.

To support all these activities, we are looking for a new colleague who can help build, deploy, and operate these advanced computing and AI services.

Your Responsibilities



Support users in operationalising AI workflows and scaling prototypes by providing hands-on guidance for pipeline setup, debugging, and optimisation.
Collaborate closely with AI experts, data engineers, and domain scientists across a diverse portfolio of AI projects in academia, industry, and the public sector to design, deploy and support scalable machine learning and GenAI workflows. Lead the technical implementation, integration, and deployment of AI solutions supporting VUB's administrative processes.
Provide third-line operational support for production AI applications and services.
Contribute to documentation, tutorials, internal knowledge sharing, and training activities.
Guide users through access procedures for the VUB HPC infrastructure and the associated EuroHPC AI Factories.



For this function, our campus in Etterbeek will serve as your home base (partially working from home is allowed).

3 - Profile

What do we expect from you?



You have a strong interest in IT and enjoy learning new technologies in a fast-paced and complex environment.
You hold a master’s degree or can demonstrate equivalent experience.
You communicate clearly and are comfortable sharing knowledge in English. Knowledge of Dutch is a plus.
You have at least a basic knowledge of Linux and are motivated to deepen your expertise.
You are comfortable scripting in Bash and Python.
You are familiar with Git and remote repository management; experience with pull request workflows is a plus.
Experience or knowledge of any of the following domains is a plus:
ML frameworks (PyTorch strongly preferred; JAX or TensorFlow are a plus)
Distributed training at scale (multi-node GPU clusters)
Containers (Apptainer/Singularity, Docker, Podman)
HPC technologies such as Slurm and MPI
Workflow orchestration tools (Nextflow, Airflow, ...)
Performance optimisation for GPUs and hardware accelerators
Cloud platforms (Azure, ...) and their AI tools (for example Azure AI Foundry)
You are a team player with a customer-oriented mindset and contribute positively to a collaborative working environment.
You are eager to learn and comfortable exploring documentation and independently researching new solutions.
You have strong problem-solving skills and can design, prototype, and implement solutions on your own to overcome technical roadblocks.
You strive for continuous improvement in both your own work and the broader team environment, and you encourage others to do the same.
As a (non-)EEA national, meet the conditions for obtaining a valid permit for VUB and comply with the VUB residence requirements. More info here .


The VUB aims to reflect a society in which everyone’s talents are valued, regardless of gender, sex, age, religion, skin colour, migrant background, disability or neurodiversity.

4 - Offer

Are you going to be our new colleague? You’ll be offered a permanent full-time contract at a salary linked to Grade 7 or 8 (scales to be consulted here ) depending on your competences and previous relevant work experience.

Benefits

At the VUB, you’re guaranteed an open, involved and diverse workplace where you are offered opportunities to (further) build on your career. As well as this, you’ll enjoy various benefits:

At the VUB, you’re guaranteed an open, involved and diverse workplace where you are offered opportunities to (further) build on your career.

As well as this, you will also enjoy various other benefits:



Extensive homeworking options, a telework allowance & tools to work from home;
An open and informal working environment where attention is paid to work-life balance, and exceptional holiday arrangements with 35 days of leave (based on a fulltime contract), closure between Christmas and New Year and 2/3 extra leave days;
Cost-free hospitalisation insurance;
Full reimbursement of your home-to-work commute with public transport according to VUB-policy, and/or compensation if you come by bike;
A wide selection of meals in our campus restaurants at attractive prices;
Excellent and affordable facilities for sport and exercise, a range of discounts via Benefits@Work (in all kinds of shops, on flights, in petrol stations, amusement parks...) and Ecocheques;
Nursery near campus, discount on holiday camps;
The space to form your job content and to continuously learn through our VUB learning platforms and training courses;
And finally: great colleagues with a healthy drive.



5 - Interested?

Is this the job you’ve been dreaming of? Apply before 30/06

Your Application Will Undergo Various Steps As Follows



Step 1: CV screening by one of our recruiters.
Step 2: A technical test/interview online in order to check your actual knowledge and potential.
Step 3: A final interview.



Do you have questions about the job content? Contact Ward Poelmans at ward.poelmans@vub.be .

Do you have questions about the selection procedure? Contact Annelies Jongert at annelies.jongert@vub.be

Would you like to know what it’s like to work at the VUB?

Go to www.jobs.vub.be and find all there is to know about our campuses, benefits, strategic goals and your future colleagues.

6 - For office use

Regulation: external funding

Contract: indefinite contract

Head unit/faculty: Directie ICT

FTE: 1

Function: ICT consultant

Pay grade group: 7-8

Recrutement type: recruitment

Title: Data Scientist – Business Analyst - MOW000699
Description:
About the job Data Scientist – Business Analyst - MOW000699Data Scientist – Business Analyst

Brussels, Belgium

Full-time (100%) | Hybrid

Start Date: 01 August 2026

Application Deadline: 18 May 2026

Contract Duration: Until 31 May 2027

We are looking for an experienced Data Scientist – Business Analyst to join an innovative data lab focused on advanced analytics, AI, and machine learning solutions within the mobility and public works domain.

In this role, you will help design, develop, and operationalise AI and data science solutions that generate actionable insights and support strategic decision-making. You will work closely with business stakeholders and technical teams to translate complex business needs into scalable AI and analytics solutions.

Key Responsibilities


Analyse complex data streams and identify valuable business insights
Design, develop, and deploy machine learning and AI solutions into production
Build predictive models using advanced ML and deep learning techniques
Develop and optimise databases, queries, and data pipelines
Translate business questions into technical and data requirements
Collaborate closely with stakeholders to define scope, requirements, and success criteria
Create intuitive visualisations and reports for business users
Support AI prototyping and experimentation initiatives
Develop AI-powered interfaces and solutions for internal services
Lead or support innovative and complex data-driven projects
Communicate findings and recommendations clearly to technical and non-technical audiences


Required Experience & Skills

Title: Machine Learning Engineer (BE)
Description:
Job Description

At CluePoints, we’re redefining how clinical trials are run. As the premier provider of Risk-Based Quality Management (RBQM) and Data Quality Oversight software, we harness advanced statistics, artificial intelligence, and machine learning to ensure the quality, accuracy, and integrity of clinical trial data, helping life sciences organizations bring safer, more effective treatments to patients faster.

We’re proud to be an ambitious, fast-growing technology scale-up with a dynamic and diverse international team representing more than 40 nationalities. Collaboration, flexibility, and continuous learning are part of our DNA.

At CluePoints, you’ll find a culture where you can grow, make an impact, and have fun along the way. Guided by our values of Care, Passion, and Smart Disruption, we’re united by a shared mission: to create smarter ways to run efficient clinical trials and deliver AI-powered insights that improve human outcomes worldwide.

We are looking for an AI Engineer to join our AI Innovation team, focused on exploring, prototyping, and operationalizing cutting-edge AI solutions for clinical data review. This role sits at the intersection of research and engineering: you will investigate emerging AI capabilities—particularly LLMs and agentic AI systems—and translate them into impactful use cases within clinical trials. While the primary focus is on innovation and experimentation, you are expected to drive selected prototypes through to robust, production-ready solutions.

Job Requirements

Main Qualifications


Master’s or PhD in Computer Science, AI, Machine Learning, or a related quantitative field
Strong programming skills in Python
Proven experience working with LLMs and modern AI frameworks (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, Meta)
Solid understanding of agentic AI concepts and orchestration frameworks (e.g.,
LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI, function calling)
Experience with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and/or hybrid AI systems
Strong software engineering fundamentals (modular design, APIs, version control with Git)


Preferred Qualifications (Nice to have)


Experience with clinical trial data (protocols, EDC, CTMS, AE/SAE reporting)
Familiarity with regulated environments (e.g., life sciences, healthcare)
Experience deploying AI/ML systems into production environments
Knowledge of vector databases, embeddings, and information retrieval systems
Background in NLP, deep learning, or applied AI research
Experience with data quality, statistical methods, or risk-based monitoring
Familiarity with GPU computing and performance optimization


Job Responsibilities

AI Innovation & Exploration


Explore and evaluate emerging AI techniques, with a focus on LLMs and agentic AI systems
Design and prototype novel AI-driven approaches for clinical data review (e.g., protocol interpretation, query generation, deviation detection, medical coding)
Translate scientific literature and industry trends into practical experimentation and prototypes
Identify new opportunities where AI can bring value within clinical research workflows


Agent & System Development


Architect and build intelligent agent systems leveraging LLMs, RAG, and tool augmentation
Integrate diverse data sources (e.g., protocols, clinical datasets, operational metadata) into AI workflows
Develop modular services, APIs, and reusable components to support AI applications
Prototype interactive interfaces (e.g., chat-based agents, copilots, APIs)


From Prototype to Production


Contribute to the industrialization of selected AI solutions, ensuring scalability and robustness
Apply best practices for evaluation, monitoring, and reproducibility of AI systems
Ensure outputs are traceable, auditable, and aligned with regulatory expectations
Collaborate with engineering teams to transition prototypes into production-ready systems


Collaboration & Thought Leadership


Work closely with clinical experts, product managers, and R&D teams to refine use cases
Communicate findings, trade-offs, and recommendations clearly to stakeholders
Stay at the forefront of AI advancements and contribute to CluePoints’ innovation strategy
Optionally contribute to publications, conferences, or internal knowledge sharing


Job Benefits

🇧🇪 What We Offer – Belgium


Health Insurance through Alan (100% hospitalisation cover, 80% ambulatory and dental)
Mobility Budget for eco-transport, housing, or car allowance (flexible 3-pillar system)
Group Insurance Plan with 6–12% employer pension contribution based on seniority
Meal Vouchers (€8/day) and Eco Vouchers for sustainable purchases
A hub-based hybrid model that blends flexibility with purpose — connecting teams through collaboration, learning, and a vibrant social culture.


Equal Opportunities & GDPR Notice

CluePoints is an equal opportunities employer. We value and respect diversity in our workforce and do not tolerate discrimination based on gender, age, disability, ethnic origin, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected ground under Belgian law.

Personal data collected as part of your application will be processed in compliance with the EU GDPR and Belgian data protection legislation.

You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data at any time by contacting privacy@cluepoints.com.

Title: AI Data Scientist
Description:
Daarom kies je voor AG




'Supporter van je leven' is niet zomaar een slogan. Het is een ambitie, een mindset. Want bij AG staan mensen centraal. Wij zorgen ervoor dat jouw job zin heeft, zodat jij zin hebt om bij ons te werken. Dag in dag uit maken we samen het verschil voor onze klanten. Zodat ze in alle rust van hun leven kunnen genieten.




Dit is jouw topjob




We zijn op zoek naar een getalenteerde AI Data Scientist met een zakelijke mindset. Je komt terecht in een dynamische omgeving die kansen biedt om analytische vaardigheden toe te passen bij het oplossen van bedrijfsproblemen in zowel België als de rest van de Ageas Groep in andere entiteiten.




Belangrijkste verantwoordelijkheden:




Je ontwerpt, ontwikkelt en implementeert data- en AI-oplossingen voor zakelijke behoeften, aan de hand van zowel interne als externe gegevensbronnen.
Je identificeert en communiceert inzichten uit gegevensanalyse om besluitvorming en probleemoplossing te ondersteunen.
Je werkt samen met cross-functionele teams om problemen met gegevens te identificeren en op te lossen.
Je controleert voortdurend AI-modellen op nauwkeurigheid en identificeert en verhelpt proactief verschilen in gegevens.




Herken je jezelf hierin?




Je bezit een masterdiploma in een kwantitatief vakgebied (bijv. statistiek, wiskunde, informatica, economie, bedrijfskunde) of een verwant vakgebied met een sterke focus op gegevensanalyse.
Je hebt minstens vijf jaar ervaring met gegevensanalyse, bij voorkeur in een bedrijfsomgeving.
Je hebt ervaring met AI & GenAI, computervisie, NLP en/of deep learning.
Je hebt een sterke kennis van gegevensanalysetechnieken en -tools, zoals Python, SQL, PBI, DataBricks.
Je hebt een sterk zakelijk inzicht en je bent in staat om zakelijke behoeften en vereisten te begrijpen en te communiceren.
Je hebt uitstekende communicatieve vaardigheden, en kan complexe gegevens op een duidelijke en beknopte manier voorstellen. Je hebt een uitstekende kennis van het Nederlands, Frans en Engels.
Je kan zowel zelfstandig als in teamverband werken, meerdere prioriteiten beheren en strakke deadlines halen.
Je bent bereid om af en toe te reizen en projecten in andere entiteiten aan te nemen.




Dit zijn jouw voordelen




Je krijgt een loonpakket met heel wat voordelen. Een deel daarvan kan je zelf samenstellen. Een leasewagen, extra verlof of verzekeringen? Jij kiest.
Jouw welzijn staat voorop. Daarom zet AG in op een sportief en inspirerend welzijnsprogramma. Toegankelijk voor iedereen.
Flexibele werkuren? Thuiswerken tot drie dagen per week? Bij AG kan het.
Je krijgt de kans om jezelf voortdurend te ontwikkelen. Met een breed aanbod aan opleidingen leer je voortdurend vaardigheden bij. Zo geef je jezelf, en je carrière een boost.
Binnen AG veranderen van functie? En dan zo je comfortzone uitbreiden? Ook dat kan. Jouw carrière, jouw keuze.
Je komt terecht in een dynamische, duurzame werkomgeving. Waar de kantoren ruimte geven om slim samen te werken. En om je volledig te kunnen geven




Ga het avontuur aan




Helemaal verkocht? Druk op de knop en solliciteer vandaag nog. Heb je ondersteuning nodig voor of tijdens het selectieproces? We helpen je graag.

Title: AI Engineer
Description:
Company Description

Sopra Steria biedt op maat gemaakte end-to-end corporate technolgy en software solutions aan die organisaties helpen gedurfde keuzes te maken en resultaten te boeken. En met succes! Met meer dan 51.000 collega’s in bijna 30 landen behoren we tot de top van de Europese digital solutions leveranciers. Succesvolle organisaties in Europa vertrouwen op onze technologie omdat we blijven streven naar innovatie, samenwerking en zakelijke ontwikkeling.

The world is how we shape it. Let’s shape it together.

Job Description

Maak deel uit van ons Data & AI-team.

Je komt terecht in een multidisciplinair team waar data engineering, architectuur, AI, analytics, governance en visualisatie samenkomen. Samen helpen we klanten om meer waarde uit hun data te halen: van het opzetten van de juiste datafundamenten tot het bouwen van dashboards, analytics-oplossingen en AI-use cases die betere beslissingen ondersteunen.

Als AI Engineer draag je hieraan bij door AI-oplossingen te ontwerpen, bouwen en industrialiseren die concrete businessproblemen oplossen en gebruikt kunnen worden in echte operationele omgevingen.

Je brengt traditionele AI en generatieve AI naar concrete praktijktoepassingen door onze klanten te helpen bij het ontwerpen, bouwen en deployen van AI-oplossingen. Je werkt aan oplossingen gaande van machine learning-modellen en NLP tot LLM’s, semantic search, RAG-oplossingen en conversational AI.

Je traint, fine-tunet, verbetert en deployt ML- en AI-modellen. Je schrijft productieklare Python-code voor online, batch- en realtime modellen, en je bouwt applicaties en softwarecomponenten die AI-gedreven use cases bruikbaar maken in de praktijk.

Je werkt nauw samen met Data Scientists, MLOps Engineers, Data Engineers, AI Architects en andere specialisten om alle onderdelen van de oplossing te integreren. Afhankelijk van het project help je nieuwe AI-oplossingen van nul op te bouwen, of ondersteun je klanten bij het beheren en verbeteren van bestaande oplossingen op middellange tot lange termijn.

Binnen ons Data & AI-team helpen we elkaar groeien en blijven we van elkaar leren. Zoek je een rol waarin je AI engineering, software development, MLOps en toegepaste innovatie kan combineren? Dan is het Data & AI-team bij Sopra Steria de juiste plek voor jou.

Qualifications

We zoeken een AI Engineer die sterke AI-kennis combineert met solide software engineering skills. Je kan verder gaan dan experimentatie en helpt AI-use cases om te zetten in betrouwbare, onderhoudbare en productieklare oplossingen.

Je beschikt over kennis en ervaring in het volgende:


Minimaal 3 jaar ervaring als Machine Learning Engineer, Data Scientist, MLOps Engineer of in een vergelijkbare functie
Ervaring met traditionele NLP, LLM’s en conversational AI, zowel in de experimenteerfase als in productie
Ervaring met vector databases voor semantisch zoeken en RAG-oplossingen in een productieomgeving
Sterke software engineering achtergrond en in staat om productieklare code te schrijven in Python
Ervaring met experiment tracking, model monitoring, LLM- en NLP-evaluatietechnieken en deploymentstrategieën
Vertrouwd met de machine learning lifecycle en MLOps- en DevOps-principes
Ervaring met ten minste één cloudprovider en goede kennis van het data-ecosysteem
In staat om anderen te coachen, technisch advies en richting te geven, en zelfstandig te werken
Master of PhD in Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Computer Engineering of een gerelateerd domein
Vloeiend in het Engels. Je spreekt daarnaast ook vloeiend Nederlands en/of Frans


Dit mag je verwachten

Bij Sopra Steria maak je deel uit van onze Data Science & AI Engineering Practice binnen het Data & AI-team: een gespecialiseerde groep consultants die klanten helpt om AI-ideeën om te zetten in praktische, schaalbare en verantwoorde oplossingen.

Ons werk gaat verder dan experimentatie. We helpen organisaties waardevolle AI-use cases te identificeren, de juiste oplossingsaanpak te bepalen, AI-systemen te bouwen en te industrialiseren, en ze te verankeren in echte business- en operationele omgevingen. Dit omvat onder meer traditionele AI, NLP, LLM’s, generatieve AI, agentic AI, trustworthy AI en MLOps.

Je bouwt je expertise verder uit in AI engineering, Python development, productieklare AI, cloud- en dataplatformen, MLOps, modelevaluatie en stakeholder collaboration. Dat doe je in een samenwerkend team waar kennisdeling, technische deep dives en coaching deel uitmaken van onze manier van werken.

Als deel van Sopra Steria profiteer je ook van sterke doorgroeimogelijkheden, zowel lokaal als internationaal, ondersteund door de Sopra Steria Academy en een breder netwerk van meer dan 51.000 professionals.

Additional Information

We bieden een aantrekkelijk voordelenpakket, waaronder:


Een waaier aan extralegale voordelen, zoals mobiliteitsopties (inclusief een bedrijfswagen), verzekeringsdekking, maaltijdcheques, ecocheques en meer.
Voortdurende leermogelijkheden via de Sopra Steria Academy zodat je je professioneel kan blijven ontwikkelen.
De kans om je collega’s van Sopra Steria te ontmoeten tijdens diverse teamevents.


Lijkt deze job iets voor jou?

Ben je klaar om met ons de wereld vorm te geven? Fantastisch! We kijken nu al uit naar jouw sollicitatie.

______

Sopra Steria is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to age, ancestry, nationality, color, family or medical leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, gender (including pregnancy), sexual orientation or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. We foster a work environment that is inclusive and respectful of all differences.
